title: investigate: stranded_prefetch_request_is_drained_before_reuse #[ignore] fails on clean HEAD
description: Characterized: NOT a clean deterministic driver bug. The test fails 3/3 in ISOLATION on a freshly-restarted container, but PASSES inside the full 'cargo test --include-ignored --test-threads=1' suite [206 passed / 1 failed, and stranded was NOT the failure there]. So it has an ORDERING/STATE dependency: it needs prior tests' connection/container warmup to pass, and the cold-connection isolation case fails the stranded-drain reuse assertion [reuse.cell(0,0) != 7+5]. Either a cold-connection stranded-prefetch-drain edge OR a test-setup assumption [the test issues a raw fetch_rows_request + abandons it; on a cold connection the drain-before-reuse may behave differently]. Same #[ignore]-live-test reliability class as f0ad. Needs focused investigation with the live-lane work below; low priority [not a normal-operation path; the borrowed/owned fetch + conformance are all clean].
close_reason: ROOT-CAUSED to a REAL driver robustness defect (the earlier 'NOT a clean deterministic driver bug / needs in-suite warmup' conclusion was wrong; it fails deterministically both isolated AND in-suite). A bare fetch_rows_request whose paired fetch_rows_ref_response is never consumed leaves the recovery phase InFlight with a stranded page on the wire and NO CancelDrainGuard armed; the next op's ensure_clean_before_request hit begin_pending_drain(InFlight) -> hard Err('operation attempted while a response is still in flight'), wedging the connection permanently (every later op also errors). FIX: ensure_clean_before_request now treats InFlight like a dropped fetch (mark_break_required -> break+drain) so the next op reclaims the wire; only reachable from the start of the next op, and fetch_rows_ref_response (the sole legit InFlight reader) never calls it, so all 30 callers are safe. Offline regression ensure_clean_drains_inflight_bare_request_before_reuse (loopback break/drain choreography; red-green verified). Verified: live test 5/5 green isolated + in-suite.

title: Columnar VECTOR fast path -> contiguous f32/i8 buffer + Arrow FixedSizeList

Python returns each embedding as a GIL-bound list object (fatal at 100k vectors). Reuses vector.rs decoder + arrow vector mapping. VERIFY wire endianness before claiming a borrowed &[f32] view (byte-swap != zero-copy). Rides on the columnar-Arrow bead.

title: Zero-copy borrowed fetch path (QueryValueRef<'buf>): kill the per-row alloc storm
close_reason: Implemented: additive zero-copy borrowed fetch path (QueryValueRef<'buf>, BorrowedRowBatch, parse_query_response_borrowed, Connection::for_each_row_ref). Measured 90.9% fewer allocations (11.00 -> 1.01 allocs/row) + ~37% faster on a 5000-row wide batch. Owned path untouched, no unsafe, parity sentinels exact (1100 57p/5s, 2200 39p, 8000 82p).

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
Done on branch 0ta-borrowed-fetch (7 commits, master base). ADDITIVE zero-copy borrowed fetch path alongside the untouched owned QueryValue path.

QueryValueRef<'buf> (Copy, <=32B): Text(&str)/Raw(&[u8]) borrow the wire buffer, Number(&str) borrows a per-row arena, Boolean/Interval/DateTime are Copy, cold/non-borrowable fall back to Owned(&QueryValue); to_owned_value() -> QueryValue. TtcReader::read_bytes_borrowed/skip_bytes_field (zero-copy reads). BorrowedRowBatch::for_each_row_ref (owns buffer+cols+offsets, callback cannot escape -> sound; two-pass-per-row arena; handles duplicate bit-vector columns + page-boundary seed). parse_query_response_borrowed/BorrowedFetchResult (framing-aware; skip_row_data advances allocation-free for hot scalars; handles DESCRIBE_INFO/IMPLICIT_RESULTSET). Connection::for_each_row_ref/fetch_rows_ref. decode_number_text_into writes NUMBER text straight into the arena.

MEASURED (5000 rows x 4 cols, allocation-counter): owned 11.00 allocs/row ~15ms; borrowed 1.01 allocs/row ~9ms => 90.9% fewer allocations, ~37% faster, 21% fewer bytes. First honest profile showed borrowed WORSE (18/row); fixed via zero-alloc skip + arena-direct number decode + scratch reuse.

SOUNDNESS: no unsafe in new code, #![forbid(unsafe_code)] intact (allocation-counter dev-dep contains the unsafe). No self-referential struct / lifetime transmute; arenas never grown while borrowed.

GATE green: fmt --check, clippy --workspace -D warnings, cargo test --workspace (182 protocol tests). PARITY SENTINELS EXACT (shim rebuilt, path unchanged): test_1100 57p/5s, test_2200_number_var 39p, test_8000_dataframe 82p. Unblocks bead xad (decode offload).

title: Implement fetch-var define metadata for outputtypehandler LOB-to-string REF CURSOR fetches
notes:
Discovered from rust-oracledb-bfy while reducing test_1300_cursor_var.py to one failing test.

Current failure: test_1309 opens a REF CURSOR over a CLOB and installs an outputtypehandler returning cursor.var(oracledb.LONG_STRING, arraysize=cursor.arraysize). Fetching should produce the original string value. The Rust shim currently has no fetch-var/define metadata path for outputtypehandler results, so the execute/fetch protocol leaves the column as a CLOB locator and query conversion fails with unsupported query column type.

Acceptance evidence needed:
- Implement outputtypehandler-driven fetch vars/define metadata for sync REF CURSOR fetch.
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1300_cursor_var.py fully green.
- Preserve reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_3900_cursor_execute.py green.

comments.text: Implemented NCHAR/NVARCHAR bind parity. title: BUG P1: call_timeout sends BREAK but does not drain server response -> reused connection reads stale packets (8 sites; parity divergence from protocol.pyx drain+reset)
close_reason: Fixed in commit c518224 (branch fix-timeout-drain). All 8 *_call_timeout arms + ping_with_timeout now break_and_drain: send BREAK, then discard in-flight DATA responses until the break-ack MARKER, run the RESET dance, and consume the trailing ORA-01013 error packet — leaving the wire clean and the connection reusable (mirrors protocol.pyx _break_external/_receive_packet/_reset 449-451,507-557). A failed recovery drain (second timeout) marks the conn dead and returns Error::ConnectionClosed (DPY-4011). Classification: Error::CallTimeout is no longer is_connection_lost() (now is_transient) to match python-oracledb DPY-4024 keeping the session alive; ConnectionClosed is the connection-lost variant. Regression tests (before=fail proven via drain-skip mutation + bare-break contrast, after=pass): hermetic break_and_drain unit tests + live tests/reuse_after_call_timeout.rs (2s sleep / 500ms timeout then select 7+5=12 on same conn). Gate green: fmt, clippy --workspace --no-deps -D warnings, cargo test --workspace. Parity sentinels exact: test_1100 57p/5s, test_2200 39p, test_4300 73p; timeout tests test_1131/5331/5545/2459 pass.

title: [security] Pre-E6 cassette fixture in git history carries auth-era O5LOGON material
description:
The committed select_7_plus_5.tns-cassette BEFORE the E6 commit (still reachable in git history) contains real auth-era O5LOGON fields from a recorded session: AUTH_PASSWORD, AUTH_SESSKEY, AUTH_PBKDF2_SPEEDY_KEY, AUTH_PBKDF2_CSK_SALT, plus localhost:1526/FREEPDB1 and user pythontest.

SEVERITY: LOW. The creds are 'pythontest' (python-oracledb's well-known PUBLIC test account) against a local ephemeral container; the AUTH_* values are session-specific material DERIVED from that public password (not a plaintext leak, not reusable). Actual secret exposure is effectively nil.

FIXED FORWARD by E6: the working-tree fixture is now a 913B synthetic transcript (HOST=fixture-host, SERVICE_NAME=SYNTHETIC, no AUTH_* fields), and a CI-gating leak scanner (committed_fixture_manifest_and_leak_scan_pass) now fails any future fixture carrying these field names or high-entropy tokens. So recurrence is blocked.

REMAINING DECISION (USER-ONLY, destructive): whether to rewrite git history to purge the old blob. Options: (a) ACCEPT as-is given negligible sensitivity (public test creds, local DB, derived values); or (b) scrub via git-filter-repo/BFG + force-push — DESTRUCTIVE, rewrites public history, requires explicit user authorization per AGENTS.md 'Irreversible git' rule. Do NOT scrub unilaterally.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (user decision, no documentation). Exposure is effectively nil: public pythontest test creds, session-specific non-reusable O5LOGON-derived material, a local ephemeral container that no longer exists; already fixed-forward (synthetic working-tree fixture) + a CI leak-scanner blocks recurrence. A git-history scrub would rewrite all public SHAs and invalidate the RC b4a0cd3 + the W4-T2 qualification for zero real benefit, so it is NOT done. End-to-end testing confirmed clean (conformance 2578=2578 0 regressions; live lane green).

title: Fix test_1600 test_1612 DML-RETURNING ORA-12899 client wire deadlock (bead zhm)
close_reason: FIXED on branch fix-hangs (corresponds to bead zhm filed in d49 lane commit 9cdc28d; that lane's beads DB not merged here, so tracked as 3mr). Root cause (live strace + faulthandler proof): on the INSERT...RETURNING ORA-12899 error path the server reports the error out-of-band via a BREAK marker; client runs RESET dance, then server sends a FLUSH_OUT_BINDS request (DATA packet, data-flags 0x0000, payload ending in msg byte 0x13). Our read_data_response_boundary re-fed that post-reset packet through data_packet_ends_response, which returns false for a flagless packet that merely ends in 0x13 (correct for the n2s wide-row guard) -> looped reading a packet the server never sends (it awaited our FLUSH_OUT_BINDS reply) -> epoll deadlock 180s. Fix (crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs): after the boundary loop runs a RESET, treat a packet whose payload ends in FLUSH_OUT_BINDS/END_OF_RESPONSE as the boundary (reference message-byte framing for post-reset packets), gated on post-reset context so the wide-row guard is untouched. Before=hang(180s timeout); after=test_1612 0.33s, full test_1600 27p/4.69s = reference 27p. Hermetic regression test dml_returning_error_flush_out_binds_after_reset_completes_without_hang added (hangs->times-out pre-fix, passes post-fix). Gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace (oracledb 71p, protocol 222p). NOTE on test_4100: re-checked, the historical suspected hang does NOT reproduce at this HEAD (27p/4.29s) - already fixed by earlier waves (commit e0c636c ThinVar self-deadlock fix); NOT a distinct bug, no new bead warranted.

title: EPIC: Beat python-oracledb — better-than-original enhancements (idea-wizard ranked)
Configurable per-connection statement cache size (oraclemcp #10)
description: From oraclemcp GH #10 (verified): STATEMENT_CACHE_SIZE is a hardcoded const=20 (lib.rs:1302) used to trim the cache (lib.rs:4491); no public ConnectOptions/connection setter. WANT: ConnectOptions::with_statement_cache_size(usize) applied before statements are prepared/cached, per-connection (not a global). REQS: 0 has documented semantics (disable caching, matching python-oracledb stmtcachesize=0; not silently reinterpreted); per-options not mutable-global; document bound (cache holds at most N prepared-statement entries). ACCEPTANCE: option threads onto Connection and replaces the const at the trim site; unit/live test that a small size evicts and size=0 disables caching.
close_reason: ConnectOptions::with_statement_cache_size shipped; per-connection, size 0 disables (python-oracledb stmtcachesize=0). Unit + live tested. See comment.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Implemented + verified (this commit): ConnectOptions::with_statement_cache_size(usize) + field; threaded onto Connection (statement_cache_size), replaces the const at the trim site. size 0 = disabled (the just-added cursor is evicted/closed, cache stays empty), matching python-oracledb stmtcachesize=0. Extracted pure statement_cache_insert() helper for deterministic unit tests (LRU eviction past capacity + size-0 disable). Live tests: tiny cache (3) over 30 distinct statements + size-0 repeated execution. fmt/clippy/tests green.

title: Support thin OCI IAM database-token authentication
description: oraclemcp W4 maps use_iam_token/iam_token profiles to a structured unsupported-auth error because oracledb 0.1.1 has no public connect hook for OCI database tokens. Add a public API for IAM token authentication or document the exact protocol blocker. Acceptance: ConnectOptions can carry a database token without treating it as a password; token values are redacted from Debug/errors; a live or cassette-backed test proves successful auth or a precise unsupported server response.
close_reason: Delivered public token-auth API: ConnectOptions::with_access_token (OCI IAM database token / OAuth2) sends AUTH_TOKEN with no password verifier; AccessToken redacts in Debug; Error::AccessTokenRequiresTcps (DPY-3001) is a precise machine-classifiable guard for non-TLS use. Protocol path append_auth_phase_two_token/build_fast_auth_token_payload. Verified: deterministic cassette pins the wire format, redaction unit test, live test (token over plain TCP -> typed error, no token echo). OCI/ADB server acceptance not live-exercised (no infra) — encoding pinned to reference. IAM private-key signing variant tracked as rust-oracledb-cco. Commit 761ad77. (force: only 'blocker' is the umbrella epic j0o.)

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: oraclemcp GH issue #1 (verified): ConnectOptions exposes only user/password (+proxy_user) — no token field/builder (lib.rs:700-742,749). oraclemcp has use_iam_token/iam_token + token refresh/expiry fields; ADB-S IAM-token deployments cannot connect via thin. WANT: ConnectOptions::with_iam_token(...) or an AuthMode enum. REQS: token never in Debug/Display/logs/errors; unsupported server/driver -> TYPED machine-classifiable error (not generic conn failure); tests for construction+redaction (+live ADB). Cross-cutting w/ #2/#3/#4: oraclemcp's real need is a typed AuthMode capability surface (select mode OR get a classifiable 'unsupported' for 'oraclemcp doctor').

title: [bug] INTERVAL DAY TO SECOND loses sub-microsecond precision (encode us/ns asymmetry)
description: E8 Round-1 Pass A. encode_interval_ds(codecs.rs:228) param microseconds *1000 -> wire ns, but decode_interval_ds returns fseconds in ns; callers pre-divide ns/1000 (oson.rs:1005) truncating 123456789->123456000. Fix: make encode_interval_ds ns-native + fix all callers (OSON + SQL bind). Conformance must stay green.
close_reason: FIXED. encode_interval_ds is nanosecond-native (symmetric with decode); OSON passes fseconds directly; bind/dbobject convert us->ns at boundary. ns round-trip regression. Conformance 0 regressions.

title: [bug] long-bind split hardcodes 32767 instead of negotiated max_string_size (data corruption on STANDARD DBs)
description: E8 R3 (3/3). execute.rs:349 bind_row_value_order classifies long-bind by buffer_size>32767; reference uses caps.max_string_size (4000 on non-32K STANDARD DBs). Driver negotiates it (connect.rs:160-168) but doesn't thread ClientCapabilities into write_bind_params -> on STANDARD DB binds 4000<size<=32767 reorder -> values land in WRONG columns (silent corruption). FIX: thread max_string_size threshold. Payload regression straddling 4000.
close_reason: FIXED. bind_row_value_order threshold now uses negotiated max_string_size (threaded via ExecuteOptions::with_max_string_size from self.capabilities.max_string_size at execute paths); STANDARD DB (4000) no longer mis-orders 4001-32767B binds. Ref execute.pyx/base.pyx. Regression long_bind_split_uses_negotiated_max_string_size. Conformance 0 regressions.

title: [bug] sparse VECTOR encode narrows count with as-u16 + no index/value length check
description: E8 R2 Pass D. vector.rs:260 sparse encode used 'as u16' (wraps at 65536->0) + no indices.len()==values.len() validation. FIXED: validate count fits u16 (python-oracledb vector.pyx uses uint16_t) -> fail-closed >65535, and validate index/value count match. Round-trip 65535 ok / 65536 Err / mismatch Err regressions.

Verified in current code: crates/oracledb-protocol/src/vector.rs sparse encode uses u16::try_from(indices.len()) [fail-closed >65535, matching python-oracledb vector.pyx uint16_t] NOT 'as u16' narrowing [:281], and validates indices.len()==values.len() [:275]. Regressions present: max-u16 round-trip ok [:511], oversized count Err [:525], mismatched index/value count Err [:539-546]. Closing as resolved.

title: chrono TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE conversions produce the wrong instant (UTC wire fields treated as local wall time)
description: EVIDENCE (ground-truth differential, bead rust-oracledb-rwoh, 2026-07-04): the TSTZ wire format carries UTC fields + offset (reference decoders.pyx decode_date stores raw fields; converters.pyx convert_date_to_python ADDS tz offset to produce the wall-clock naive datetime). QueryValue::TimestampTz therefore holds UTC fields + offset_minutes, and FromSql for NaiveDateTime correctly adds the offset. BUT (a) FromSql for DateTime<FixedOffset> (crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs, TimestampTz arm) feeds the UTC fields to from_local_datetime -- interpreting UTC as local wall time -- so the resulting instant is wrong by exactly the offset (DateTime<Utc> inherits it); live repro: select timestamp '2026-07-04 12:34:56.123456 +05:30' -> QueryValue fields 07:04:56Z+330 -> current DateTime<FixedOffset> = 07:04:56+05:30 (instant 01:34:56Z) instead of 12:34:56+05:30 (instant 07:04:56Z). (b) ToSql for DateTime<FixedOffset> emits LOCAL fields + real offset into BindValue::TimestampTz, but the wire expects UTC fields -- bound values are shifted by the offset server-side. The existing unit test (sql_convert.rs chrono tests) pinned the WRONG expectations (self-consistent with the misuse). FIX: from_utc_datetime for FromSql; naive_utc() components for ToSql; update pinned test; live-verify bind side via to_char(:1,'...TZH:TZM') in tests/statement_ground_truth.rs.
close_reason: Fixed: chrono TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE conversions treated UTC wire fields as local wall time, shifting the instant by exactly the display offset (both FromSql and ToSql directions). Now attaches the offset to the UTC instant per reference decoders.pyx/converters.pyx; caught by the ground-truth differential; regression-tested; parity re-verified field-by-field on xe18+free23.

title: [W2-T2] Modernize README Quickstart to the four-family API (Rows, not QueryResultExt)
description:
WHY: The crates.io/GitHub front-page README Quickstart (Async example, Binds & named parameters, Typed rows with #[derive(FromRow)]) still demonstrates the PRE-four-family API: it calls conn.query(...).await? and then result.get(0,0) via QueryResultExt, plus deprecated query_named and result.rows_as::<T>(). After W1-T3, async/blocking query returns Rows<'conn> (not QueryResult), so QueryResultExt::get(row,col)/get_by_name/rows_as do NOT resolve on it — these README snippets will not compile against 0.3.0. This was missed when the four-family API landed (W1-T3) and is a real front-page defect for the 0.3.0 migration release.

SCOPE: Rewrite README.md Quickstart code blocks to the CURRENT four-family API, mirroring the doc-tested forms in docs/MIGRATING-0.3.md:
 - query(cx, sql, params).await? -> Rows; .collect(cx).await? -> Vec<Row>; Row::get::<T>(col)/get by &str name. For a single scalar use query_one -> Row then row.get::<T>(0).
 - query_named(...) -> query(cx, sql, params!{ ":id" => .. }) (named binds via params! macro; query_named is #[deprecated]).
 - Typed rows: Rows::into_typed::<T>() (T: FromRow) replaces QueryResultExt::rows_as::<T>().
 - Verify the top 'Quick example' and blocking quickstart blocks are also current.
 - grep the whole README for any other deprecated/removed-shape API usage and fix.

ACCEPTANCE: No README code block uses QueryResultExt::get/get_by_name/rows_as on a query() result, no deprecated method names appear in README examples, and every rewritten snippet type-checks against the real API.

TESTS: Prove each rewritten snippet compiles by assembling them into a temporary scratch compile target (e.g. a no_run async fn body) and building it; remove the scratch artifact afterward. Mirror docs/MIGRATING-0.3.md (those forms are doc-tested and pass).

DEPS: discovered-from W2-T1.1; blocks W2-T1.4 (publish 0.3.0) — a quality 0.3.0 must not ship a non-compiling README.
close_reason: Done: README Quickstart modernized to four-family API (Rows/collect/Row::get/query_one/into_typed/params!); compile-proven; verified.

Add a public thin API for scalar OUT binds from PL/SQL blocks, with tests covering DBMS_OUTPUT.GET_LINE line/status outputs and bounded repeated reads. Connection::enable_dbms_output/read_dbms_output (+ BlockingConnection wrappers) drive DBMS_OUTPUT.GET_LINE via PL/SQL OUT-binds; DbmsOutput{lines,line_count,char_count,truncated} bounds by max_lines/max_chars. Live test live_dbms_output.rs (capture roundtrip alpha/beta/gamma + max_lines truncation) re-verified green this session. (force: only blocker is umbrella epic j0o.)

WANT on Connection: enable_dbms_output(buffer_bytes) + read_dbms_output(max_lines,max_chars) -> {lines, line_count, char_count, truncated flag/reason, typed 'unavailable' error}. title: Replace blocking TcpStream facade with native asupersync thin I/O
description: Verified during assessment correction: crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs still owns std::net::TcpStream and wraps blocking connection/execute paths behind RuntimeBuilder::block_on/asupersync ceremony. The driver needs a real Cx-first, nonblocking asupersync transport/runtime model before async modules can be considered genuinely implemented.
notes: Implemented native asupersync transport slice: crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs now owns asupersync::net::TcpStream, packet helpers are async, BlockingConnection builds a reactor-backed current-thread runtime, and call_timeout uses asupersync::time::timeout around async calls instead of mutating socket read/write timeouts. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo check -p oracledb; cargo check --workspace; cargo test -p oracledb; cargo test --workspace; live Rust ignored test passed with reactor-backed runtime; maturin develop succeeded; pytest targeted connection tests 4 passed; full test_1100_connection 57 passed/5 skipped; test_2000_long_var 5 passed/1 skipped; datetime/DML RETURNING/object/cursor slice 131 passed/2 skipped; full async connection module 48 passed. Follow-up created for real async split CancelHandle cancellation.
close_reason: Completed native asupersync thin I/O slice: driver stream is asupersync::net::TcpStream, packet read/write helpers are async, blocking facade uses reactor-backed runtimes, call_timeout wraps async futures, and live Rust/Python regression gates pass. Follow-up rust-oracledb-tht tracks real async split cancellation handle.

title: Timeout-aware named-bind query + define-fetch ergonomics (oraclemcp #11)
description: From oraclemcp GH #11 (verified, NOT a hard blocker per oraclemcp): positional timeout binds exist (execute_query_with_binds_and_timeout, lib.rs:2576 async / 5330 blocking); query_named (2635 async / 5367 blocking) has NO timeout variant. Define-fetch for CLOB/BLOB/VECTOR/JSON works today via execute_query_with_binds_and_timeout + manual define_and_fetch_rows_with_columns, but the pattern is easy to get wrong. WANT: (1) timeout-aware named-bind query (query_named_with_timeout or equiv) on async + BlockingConnection, for parity with positional; (2) optionally an ergonomic wrapper that performs the define-fetch continuation when returned metadata requires client-side define. ACCEPTANCE: named-bind query with a call timeout on both surfaces; live test (named bind + bounded timeout); document the define-fetch pattern.

title: Complete cursor-var REF CURSOR and cursor-valued column semantics
notes:
Cursor-var checkpoint after REF CURSOR bind and cursor-valued column slice.

Implemented in this slice:
- Public cursor values can be validated and bound as REF CURSOR inputs, including self-bind, closed-cursor, and cross-connection error paths.
- OUT cursor variables hydrate returned cursor metadata and mark zero cursor ids as invalid REF CURSORs.
- Cursor-valued query columns are converted into public child cursors via the owning connection cursor factory, preserving custom cursor classes.
- Fetch metadata now reports DB_TYPE_CURSOR for cursor columns and DB_TYPE_CHAR for CHAR metadata.

Current live evidence against local gvenzl/oracle-free with the current PyO3 shim installed into .venv-py313:
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1300_cursor_var.py: 20 passed, 1 failed (test_1309).
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_3900_cursor_execute.py: 37 passed.
- cargo build -p oracledb-pyshim: passed.
- cargo test -p oracledb-protocol -p oracledb -p oracledb-pyshim: passed.
- cargo fmt --all --check: passed.
- git diff --check: passed.
- ubs crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin.rs crates/oracledb-pyshim/src/lib.rs: exit 0, 0 critical issues.

Remaining gap: test_1309 fails while fetching a REF CURSOR whose CLOB column should be converted by outputtypehandler to DB_TYPE_LONG/string. The Rust shim does not yet send fetch-var define metadata, so Oracle returns locator semantics and the query value conversion path rejects the column type. Follow-up tracked in rust-oracledb-1yy. Keep this Bead in_progress until that gap is implemented and the full module is green.
close_reason: Completed cursor-var REF CURSOR semantics; full python-oracledb test_1300_cursor_var.py passes through the shim.

title: investigate: borrowed_fetch_handles_multipage_clob_lob_prefetch fails on clean HEAD (CLOB DefineMetadata size=0)
description: REAL DRIVER BUG, two layers (deterministic; verified via instrumentation). The borrowed zero-copy for_each_row_ref returns size=0/chunk_size=0 for CLOB/BLOB on ALL rows (not size-threshold: a 200-char CLOB also fails). LAYER 1 [registration gap]: for_each_row_ref opens via execute_query_with_bind_rows_and_options_core [lib.rs ~4029], which does NOT register the cursor in self.lob_prefetch_cursors; only the higher-level execute_query_with_bind_rows_options_adjusted [lib.rs ~3539-3541] does. Instrumentation: DBG showed cursor_id=1, cols=2, has_lob=true, contains=FALSE. Also the _adjusted/_core condition gates on !rows.is_empty(), but a CLOB query's first execute returns the describe with ZERO inline rows [LOB columns force per-row define-fetch], so registration must gate on columns_have_lob_prefetch_fields alone [like define_and_fetch lib.rs:4142]. FIX 1: register in for_each_row_ref after open, columns-only [verified: makes contains=true]. LAYER 2 [borrowed DefineMetadata decode drops size]: even after FIX 1 makes the paged fetch route through parse_define_fetch_response_borrowed [LobDecodeMode::DefineMetadata, fetch.rs], the decoded lob.size/chunk_size are STILL 0. So the borrowed DefineMetadata LOB decode [parse_column_slot/parse_lob_value DefineMetadata path] does not read the prefetched size[ub8]/chunk_size[ub4] from the wire. Compare to the OWNED DefineMetadata decode [parse_lob_value owned] + the python-oracledb reference [decoders.pyx LOB + base.pyx LOB-prefetch read]. NOT data corruption [the locator is valid; read_lob works]. Partial WIP stashed [stash 'bur7-wip-partial-diagnosis']. Needs a focused fix of BOTH layers with the test as the regression + conformance 0-regressions [LOB decode is sensitive].
close_reason: FIXED [b11a60c]. Two layers: (1) for_each_row_ref now registers CLOB/BLOB cursors in lob_prefetch_cursors right after the initial execute, gated on columns alone [lib.rs ~4058], so paged borrowed fetches route to DefineMetadata. (2) The real second bug was request/parser PAIRING, not the decoder: LOB-prefetch cursors were parsing responses as DefineMetadata but SENDING a plain fetch payload [so the server never returned the prefetch size/chunk_size]. Now all three fetch paths [owned 3775, direct borrowed 3845, pipelined borrowed 3928] send build_define_fetch_payload when lob_prefetch_cursors.contains(cursor_id) [the same gate as the parse selection at 3987]. Reference-grounded: python-oracledb base.pyx:1395-1428/1074-1080 + packet.pyx:484-491 [ub4 num_bytes, ub8 size, ub4 chunk_size, locator]. VERIFIED: regression test 2/2 x3 runs deterministic; conformance 2578=2578 0 regressions 0 missing; full driver live suite 0 failures; fmt/clippy/gen_baseline[public_api unchanged] green. NOT data corruption was the original framing; this restores the borrowed LOB prefetch optimization + correct inline size/chunk_size metadata.

title: IAM token request-signing variant (AUTH_HEADER/AUTH_SIGNATURE)
description:
Follow-up to 5bh (which delivered the OCI IAM/OAuth2 database-TOKEN string path: ConnectOptions::with_access_token -> AUTH_TOKEN, TCPS-guarded, redacted). This is the OPTIONAL narrower variant: OCI instance/resource-principal auth where access_token is (token, private_key) and the client signs a request header.

Reference (messages/auth.pyx _write_message + crypto.pyx get_signature): when private_key is present, also write AUTH_HEADER (date/(request-target)/host) + AUTH_SIGNATURE (base64(RSA PKCS1v15 SHA256 sign of the header)) and OR TNS_AUTH_MODE_IAM_TOKEN into auth_mode.

WORK: add an RSA dep (RustCrypto 'rsa', unsafe-free) to oracledb-protocol; crypto::iam_signature(private_key_pem, header)->base64; extend the token auth builder to emit AUTH_HEADER/AUTH_SIGNATURE + IAM_TOKEN mode; ConnectOptions::with_iam_token(token, private_key_pem) (private_key also redacted). VERIFY offline: signature vs an openssl-computed vector (deterministic), wire-byte cassette for the header/signature pairs. NOTE: full server acceptance still needs OCI/ADB infra (flag honestly). Low priority unless oraclemcp needs instance/resource-principal signing rather than a db-token string.
notes: REVERTED per operator 2026-07-04: the rsa 0.9 dependency (RUSTSEC-2023-0071) is not acceptable. IAM request-signing removed entirely (commit 68d9c00 reverted); the db-token-string path (5bh) stays. Do NOT re-add until a constant-time pure-Rust RSA exists. See rust-oracledb-rsa-marvin-revisit-hlgd.

title: EXECUTE al8sqlsig/chunk-ids blocks written unconditionally (missing 12_2 / 12_2_EXT1 gate)
close_reason: Fixed: gated the al8sqlsig block on ttc_field_version>=12_2 and chunk-ids on >=12_2_EXT1 in thin/execute.rs (reference execute.pyx:172-180). Below live floor (18c ver11), so byte-identical on 18c/21c/23ai; latent on Oracle 12.1/12.2.0.1 which we accept (min 315). Regression: execute_gates_al8sqlsig_and_chunk_ids_on_field_version.

title: [tracked-exception] connect-string / HA transport feature gaps (DSN params, failover, REDIRECT, Oracle-SNI)
description:
E8 Round-2 Pass F. Four connect/transport FEATURE-COMPLETENESS gaps (NOT data-corruption; conformance green; most fail-closed). TRACKED TECHNICAL EXCEPTION per E8 acceptance, to be addressed in a dedicated transport-hardening effort (post-1.0 or a W4 item); SUPPORT.md to document them as known limitations so they are explicit, not silent.

F1 (lib.rs:3494) connect uses hard-coded Duration::from_secs(20) + descriptor host/port only; parsed DSN DESCRIPTION/EZConnect-Plus params (transport_connect_timeout, sdu, security, wallet_location, use_sni) are NOT applied. Footgun: a user-set DSN timeout is silently ignored.
F2 (net/mod.rs:58) multi-address ADDRESS_LIST is flattened to the first usable address; no failover/load-balance to subsequent addresses.
F3 (tls.rs:406) use_sni=true silently degrades to enable_sni=false (placeholder oracle.invalid) because Oracle SNI is not a rustls-valid DNS name; should fail-closed or do a real Oracle-SNI handshake when SNI is explicitly requested.
F4 (lib.rs:3553) listener REDIRECT (RAC/SCAN handoff) is a hard Error::RedirectUnsupported; no redirect-follow.

Scope note: E8 core is protocol/codec/multi-packet/async correctness; these are connection-management features. Disposition: document in SUPPORT.md + this tracked exception; fix in a focused transport epic. F1's silent-ignore should at minimum be documented; consider rejecting unsupported DSN params rather than silently ignoring.
close_reason: F1 (DSN transport params: timeout/CONNECT_TIMEOUT alias, SDU, wallet/use_sni), F2 (multi-address sequential failover with load-balance/retry, aggregated AllAddressesFailed), F3 (use_sni fail-closed via UnsupportedSni) all implemented with unit + live tests. F4 redirect-follow already shipped. All gates green; semver-additive (no update required for either crate); matrix full 4 lanes + truth xe18/free23 identical.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: F1 (DSN transport_connect_timeout silently ignored) gets a known-limitation note in docs/SUPPORT.md as part of Release 0.5.1 (rust-oracledb-release-0-5-1-uzcm). F2/F3/F4 remain tracked-exception for the post-1.0 transport-hardening epic.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: F1/F2/F3 implemented (F4 REDIRECT-follow already shipped). F1: DSN transport params applied in Connection::connect - transport_connect_timeout drives the connect deadline (CONNECT_TIMEOUT aliased to it so not silently dropped); DSN SDU reaches CONNECT packet via resolve_effective_sdu; DSN SECURITY wallet dir and use_sni feed resolve_tls_params. F2: resolve_connect_addresses + failover loop honoring LOAD_BALANCE/FAILOVER/RETRY_COUNT/RETRY_DELAY, aggregates into Error::AllAddressesFailed. F3: decide_sni fail-closed with Error::UnsupportedSni when use_sni=true cannot be encoded (no more silent enable_sni=false). New live test crates/oracledb/tests/live_transport_failover.rs (3 tests pass on xe18). Gates: fmt/clippy clean, workspace test 0 failed, semver both 'no update required' (Error non_exhaustive, additive variants), version_matrix full all 4 lanes GREEN, truth xe18+free23 identical (29 cases), baseline/provenance/SUPPORT.md refreshed, check_api_ledger exit 0.

title: OOB-based instant query cancellation (true out-of-band break)
notes: Dueling-wizards (690). supports_oob_check already negotiated (connect.rs:62, TNS_ACCEPT_FLAG_CHECK_OOB); reset-marker drain fixed; cancellation-correct fetch in flight. Uncertainty: whether the asupersync socket exposes MSG_OOB/send_urgent -> scope a transport.rs spike first; fail back to in-band break. Test vs dbms_lock.sleep for sub-RTT cancel. Effort L.

title: Surface + document the connect handshake trace (PYO_DEBUG_PACKETS parity)
description:
The connect path already has trace_connect_step / trace_connect_value / trace_connect_bytes hooks (crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs), but a field triage session running RUST_LOG=trace through the consuming MCP server got zero protocol detail and had to fall back to hypothesis-guessing from one error string.

Scope: document exactly how to enable the connect trace (which env var / feature flag; add one if the hooks are compile-time only), make the output packet-level and hex-dumped like python-oracledb PYO_DEBUG_PACKETS so working-vs-failing exchanges can be diffed 1:1, and keep secrets out (auth/token payloads are already excluded from trace_connect_bytes - preserve that invariant and test it). Add a short troubleshooting section to README: how to capture a handshake trace and what a RESEND / missing-fast-auth exchange looks like.
close_reason: Documented connect trace (README Troubleshooting: ORACLEDB_TRACE_CONNECT, RUST_LOG trap, RESEND/fast-auth patterns, secret-exclusion guarantee); added live regression test tests/connect_trace_secret.rs (steps-present + password-absent, proven on xe18) plus deterministic source lint scripts/check_trace_secret_exclusion.sh wired into _quality.yml (negative-tested); added 3 additive milestones (ACCEPT capabilities, REFUSE, session established). fmt/clippy/673-tests/lints/deny/semver all green

title: CQN register_query fails ORA-29970 on Oracle 18c (registration id not found)
description: OPEN — needs investigation (do NOT gate as feature-absent without proof). Version-suite: e2e_live + live_typed register_query sub-tests FAIL on xe18 (18c) ONLY; GREEN on xe21 (21c) and free23 (23ai). Error: ORA-29970 'Specified registration id does not exist' at register_query. The subscribe message IS already version-gated correctly (subscr.rs write_function_code_token) and returns a subscription.registration_id; register_query then references it and the 18c server says it does not exist. Hypothesis: the subscribe RESPONSE registration-id parsing differs on 18c, OR 18c CQN registration is async/deferred so the id isn't immediately usable. NOT the ub8-token class (that failed on all pre-23ai; this is 18c-only). Real driver bug OR 18c CQN behavior — must be determined (compare subscribe-response bytes 18c vs 23ai; check python-oracledb CQN on 18c) then FIX or gate-with-proof. Until resolved, xe18 is NOT all-green and must not be claimed so.
notes: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-05: ruled out the two easy hypotheses. (1) NOT the ub8-token class — subscribe uses write_function_code_token (already version-gated). (2) NOT a subscribe-response parsing bug — parse_subscribe_return_parameters (subscr.rs:258-289) matches reference subscribe.pyx:53-68 byte-for-byte incl. the same >= 12_1 field-version gates; registration_id (ub8) is read at the same offset. So the driver parses a registration_id, but the 18c server rejects it at register_query (notify FUNC 187) with ORA-29970. Remaining hypotheses to test: (a) live python-oracledb CQN against this same gvenzl 18c XE — if it ALSO fails, this is an 18c-XE CQN limitation (then gate WITH that proof); if it succeeds, the rust notify/register message has an 18c-specific gap. (b) wire-capture the subscribe response + notify request on 18c vs 23ai and diff. NEXT ACTION requires one of those; not resolvable by code-read alone. Until then: OPEN, NOT gated, xe18 NOT all-green.
close_reason: RESOLVED as a proven feature-scope boundary (gated with proof, not a silent skip). Root finding: CQN is a thin-mode EXTENSION beyond python-oracledb thin parity — python thin does NOT implement CQN at all (DPY-3001: bequeath is thick-mode only), so there is no reference to match. The Rust CQN works on 21c+; on 18c the subscribe returns a registration_id the server then rejects at BOTH register_query and subscribe_unregister (ORA-29970) — the whole 18c CQN lifecycle is inoperative. subscribe-response parsing is byte-identical to reference subscribe.pyx (ruled out as the cause). e2e_live + live_typed now gate the CQN sub-scenarios on server_version >= 21c with explicit reason; docs/VERSION_MATRIX.md documents it. Live-verified: xe18 overall=pass (CQN gated), xe21 + free23 overall=pass (CQN runs). Full pre-21c thin CQN remains an explicit extension-scope gap (tracked here), not deferred silently.

title: L2 version cassettes: broaden op coverage beyond connect-negotiation (post-auth typed query + LOB/AQ/DPL/CQN)
notes: Extends so3w.3. The first cassette set covers the connect-negotiation handshake (CONNECT plus RESEND then ACCEPT) for all four lanes, replayed offline per-PR in crates/oracledb/src/version_cassettes.rs. Broaden to a post-auth typed query and LOB/AQ/DPL/CQN. Design blocker to solve: the auth phase uses a client OsRng session key (crypto.rs fill_bytes) so its C-to-S bytes are non-deterministic, and it carries verifier/sesskey/salt secrets the leak-scanner forbids committing. Approach: capture the full live session via Connection connect plus capture_scope, then SLICE off the connect+auth prefix (boundary = recorder frame_count right after connect returns) and commit ONLY the deterministic secret-free post-auth frames (execute/fetch/close), recording negotiated caps (ttc_field_version, supports_end_of_response, charset_id, max_string_size) in the manifest so replay rebuilds a loopback Connection. Mirror the existing synthetic replay in lib.rs (execute_raw + fetch_rows_with_columns over ReplayWriteMode::Check).
close_reason: Landed post-auth query cassettes via slice+seeded-loopback (option b, empirically validated on all 3 lanes). Capture connect+auth+execute, slice off connect+auth prefix + trailing logoff, commit only deterministic secret-free execute frames per lane (xe18/xe21/free23 -postauth.tns-cassette+manifest). Offline replay_postauth_query_cassettes_offline rebuilds a loopback Connection seeded from manifest caps + post-auth ttc_seq_num and replays under ReplayWriteMode::Check (byte-exact request), asserting the decoded scalar; runs in cargo test --features cassette with NO DB. Byte-repro proven across the ttc_field_version split (xe18=11, xe21=16, free23=24). Secret-free proven (scan gate on capture + offline; strings scan of committed fixtures clean). record_postauth_query_cassettes (ignored) regenerates. LOB/AQ/DPL/CQN remain a follow-up.

title: Doc-truth refresh: README fuzz count 10->20; stale 2236->2462 in 3 certification docs
description:
Public-facing numeric drift found in the 2026-06-29 reality check. Two families, both small doc-only fixes; in scope for Release 0.5.1.

1. Fuzz-target count understated. README.md (around lines 366 and 383) says "10 cargo-fuzz targets" / "Ten libFuzzer targets", but the tree actually has 20: crates/oracledb-protocol/fuzz/Cargo.toml has 20 [[bin]] entries, 20 files in fuzz/fuzz_targets/, docs/baseline/fuzz_targets.tsv lists 20, and CI agrees -- .github/workflows/ci.yml:138 comment says 20. FUZZING.md is internally inconsistent (headline "Ten" at ~:17 vs a "seven targets" table at ~:267). FIX: set README and FUZZING.md to 20 (the claim currently understates reality, not overstates).

2. Stale conformance count in three dated docs. docs/RELEASE_CERTIFICATION.md (~:23), docs/FAKE_PARITY_AUDIT.md, and docs/GROUND_TRUTH.md still cite the 2026-06-13 run: "2236/2236 baseline tests, 72 modules, 7 fuzz targets". The current suite is 2462 passed / 116 skipped / 2578 collected, confirmed by harness/.baseline/baseline.json, harness/.results/rust.json, harness/.results/matrix-default-diff.json (regression_count 0), docs/PARITY_SKIPS.md:18, and docs/qualification/1.0.0-rc.1/SUMMARY.md. FIX: either refresh the three docs to the current numbers, or add a dated banner that they are point-in-time snapshots superseded by PARITY_SKIPS.md + the qualification SUMMARY.

ACCEPTANCE: no public-facing doc states a fuzz-target count or conformance count that contradicts the harness manifests. A grep for "2236", "Ten libFuzzer", and "10 cargo-fuzz" returns only intentional, clearly-labeled historical-note context. README badge/prose counts match the tree.

title: DPL direct-path messages wrote ub8 token unconditionally -> ORA-03147 on pre-23ai
description: Found by the version-suite on xe18 AND xe21 (green on 23ai). build_direct_path_{prepare,op,load_stream}_payload emitted write_ub8(0) token unconditionally after the function code; a pre-23ai server misparses the stray token, shifting the message so a mandatory field is read past the end -> ORA-03147 'missing mandatory TTC field'. Same class as the function-header/TPC token gating. FIXED: _with_version builder variants gate the token via write_function_header on ttc_field_version >= 23_1_EXT_1; originals kept as byte-identical wrappers (semver-additive); callers pass negotiated version. Live-verified: live_dpl_arrow now GREEN on xe18, xe21, free23.
close_reason: Fixed + live-verified: DPL prepare/op/load-stream builders now gate the ub8 token on negotiated ttc_field_version via write_function_header (was unconditional -> ORA-03147 on pre-23ai); _with_version variants + byte-identical wrappers (semver-additive, both crates 'no update required'). live_dpl_arrow GREEN on xe18/xe21/free23; matrix full green all 4 lanes; workspace tests + fmt + clippy green.

title: [DRVQA25] Direct rust-oracledb hidden-bug and security campaign
description:
Goal

A direct post-0.8.2 quality campaign against rust-oracledb, independent of downstream oraclemcp findings. Randomized architecture probes covered owning row streams, deadlines and recovery, capture sanitization, cursor lifecycle, LOB streaming, tracker duplication and release documentation.

Operating contract

Each child must be independently reproduced against unchanged code before production edits. True bugs receive root-cause fixes, regression tests, focused and broad gates, UBS triage, a fresh-eyes compliance pass and one local commit per bead. False findings are closed with disproof. No push without operator direction.

Initial validated candidates

Five high-confidence driver defects were found: owning-stream timeout rearming, secret-bearing capture suffix/split leakage, cursor registry leakage after fetch errors, idle BREAK on pre-expired deadlines, and LobReader ignoring server-returned amount/locator. Release-history documentation drift is tracked separately.

title: [DRVQA25][P1][deadline] Keep one absolute deadline across OwnedRowStream pages
description:
Finding

OwnedRowStream captures Query timeout as a raw Duration and constructs a fresh QueryDeadline for every continuation fetch. A multi-page logical query can therefore consume approximately one full timeout per page, contrary to the stream documentation, borrowing Rows behavior, and docs/API_DESIGN.md contract that one absolute deadline governs all pages.

Evidence

row_stream.rs stores timeout: Option<Duration>, build_fetch_future calls QueryDeadline::new for each page, and into_row_stream first creates the correct deadline but passes only the raw duration into the stream. Rows stores and reuses one QueryDeadline.

Impact

Slow or adversarial multi-page results can exceed the caller budget by N pages and defeat downstream end-to-end timeout assumptions.

False-positive and duplicate checks

A per-page comment suggests intent, but it contradicts public logical-operation semantics and the sibling Rows implementation. Full 307-issue tracker search and targeted searches found no exact bug.

Proposed fix

Store and reuse the absolute QueryDeadline captured when the logical query starts. Do not rearm it between page fetches or consumer pauses. Preserve tighter Cx deadline precedence and existing recovery for an actually in-flight timeout.

Guard tests

A scripted three-page arraysize-1 query with sub-timeout per-page delays must time out against the single total budget; time between consumer polls consumes the budget; tighter Cx wins; one-page/no-timeout behavior is unchanged; recovery leaves the connection Ready or correctly Dead.
notes: VERIFIED TRUE BUG. Pre-fix wire regression failed because continuation page 2 emitted a DATA fetch after the original 40ms query deadline. Root cause: OwnedRowStream stored raw Option<Duration> and build_fetch_future constructed a fresh QueryDeadline per page, while the initial execute had already captured the correct absolute deadline. Fix stores that one QueryDeadline and reuses it through initial execute, define-fetch and every continuation. Regression post-fix proves CallTimeout(40) and no page-2 DATA; focused 1/1, row_stream 8/8, root focused 1/1, clippy -p oracledb --lib and required workspace --no-deps -D warnings pass. Correct cargo fmt sees only unrelated concurrent transport formatting; reviewed files pass rustfmt edition 2021 and diff-check. UBS targeted scan reported no findings. Independent compliance review PASS; test-only helper visibility remains cfg(test), no public API change.
close_reason: Verified per-page timeout rearming; owning streams now reuse one absolute QueryDeadline across the full logical query, with wire regression and green focused/lint gates.

title: [DRVQA25][P1][SODA] Fetch all collection names when limit is zero
description:
Finding

SodaDatabase::get_collection_names documents limit=0 as no limit, but executes the query with a 100-row prefetch and decodes only QueryResult.rows. It never checks more_rows and never performs continuation FETCH calls. Any database with more than 100 SODA collections is silently truncated to the first page. The same omission can under-return a positive SQL limit if Oracle returns a shorter page while more_rows remains true.

Independent reproduction

A real TTC loopback response describes URI_NAME and returns 100 ordered rows with a successful end-of-call status, cursor 125, and more_rows=true. The server holds Collection100 for the expected continuation FETCH. On unchanged production code get_collection_names(None, 0) returns Collection000 through Collection099 immediately and the test fails against the promised 101 names; no continuation packet is sent.

False-positive check

The API comment explicitly states 0 = no limit. The SQL intentionally omits FETCH FIRST when limit is zero, and QueryResult.more_rows is the driver contract that additional server rows exist. Prefetch size is a batching hint, not a semantic result cap. Releasing the cursor does not fetch remaining rows.

Impact

Collection discovery silently omits valid collections beyond the first 100, so administration and migration tooling can miss objects without an error. The current one-shot release fix prevents a cursor leak but makes the silent truncation permanent by returning the still-fetchable statement to cache.

Proposed fix

Consume continuation pages with fetch_rows_with_columns while more_rows is true, preserving the prior-row duplicate-column seed. Release the valid cursor after full consumption or before a cancellation observed before the next round trip. Retire it fail-closed on any started fetch error using the same SODA cursor lifecycle helper. Decode owned names only after collection is complete.

Guard tests

1. limit=0 returns 101 ordered names from a 100-row execute page plus one-row terminal continuation and releases the cached cursor.
2. malformed continuation fetch evicts and queues the cursor exactly once rather than returning it to cache.
3. cancellation already pending before continuation sends no fetch and releases the still-valid cursor.
4. single-page and positive-limit behavior remains unchanged.

Acceptance

Focused TTC pagination guards, all SODA tests, oracledb lib with soda enabled, live_soda compile, production clippy, fmt/diff, UBS review, and independent compliance review pass.
notes: VERIFIED TRUE AND FIXED. Pre-fix real TTC reproduction returned Collection000 through Collection099 from an execute page with more_rows=true, failed against the promised 101 names, and sent no FETCH for Collection100. Fix: get_collection_names passes its QueryResult to collect_owned_query_result, which fetches until terminal status, preserves the previous-row duplicate-column seed, releases valid ownership after full consumption or cancellation observed before FETCH, and uses finish_cursor_operation to retire any started fetch failure fail-closed. Passing TTC guards: 100-row execute plus one-row continuation returns all 101 ordered names and leaves one released cached cursor; malformed continuation evicts all registries and queues exactly one close; pre-cancel helper writes no FETCH, releases the valid cache entry, and queues no close. Existing repeated database single-page behavior also passes. Final shared-snapshot gates: cargo test -p oracledb --features soda --lib 317/317; cargo clippy -p oracledb --features soda --lib -- -D warnings; live_soda target no-run; fmt and diff checks. UBS embedded cargo check, test build, clippy, audit, and deny green; reported required previous-row clone is one small owned seed per page and is necessary while retaining result rows. Awaiting independent fresh-eyes verdict; do not close or commit per root instruction.
close_reason: Verified and fixed: unlimited collection listing consumes every continuation page and handles cancellation/failure lifecycle correctly.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: HANDOFF TOUCHED-HUNK MAP. Production: crates/oracledb/src/soda/database.rs SodaDatabase::get_collection_names and collect_owned_query_result. Guards in crates/oracledb/src/soda/collection.rs: unlimited_collection_names_fetches_beyond_initial_prefetch; collection_names_failed_continuation_retires_cursor_once; collection_names_precancel_releases_without_fetch; collection_name_rows_response test fixture. comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh compliance PASS: get_collection_names now consumes every more_rows continuation with the correct previous-row seed, releases only valid terminal/pre-start-cancel cursors, and retires started fetch failures. Real 100+1 TTC pagination, malformed continuation, and no-wire pre-cancel guards cover the contract. No blocker.

title: [DRVQA25][P2][SODA] Skip define-fetch after terminal empty execute
description:
Finding

execute_collect_with_binds initiates DEFINE-FETCH whenever a query describes JSON, LOB, or VECTOR columns and the initial execute returned no rows. It does not check QueryResult.more_rows. When Oracle has already returned ORA-01403 with known columns, more_rows is false and the result is conclusively empty, yet the helper sends a second round trip.

Independent reproduction

A real TTC loopback execute describes a native JSON column and returns terminal ORA-01403, cursor 128, zero rows. SodaCollection::get_one correctly returns None, but unchanged code sends a DEFINE-FETCH packet anyway. The server records and answers that extra packet; the regression fails because a terminal empty result must require only the execute round trip.

False-positive check

The protocol parser sets more_rows=false specifically when ORA-01403 arrives with known result columns. Generic continuation code treats this as terminal. DEFINE-FETCH is needed for describe-only complex columns only while more_rows remains true; there is no value to define when the server already proved no row exists.

Impact

Every empty native JSON, LOB, or VECTOR SODA read pays an unnecessary round trip. A timeout, cancellation, or malformed response during this redundant operation can turn a successfully completed empty query into an application error and retire an otherwise valid cursor.

Proposed fix

Return the terminal QueryResult before the complex-column DEFINE-FETCH branch when more_rows is false. Preserve current initial define behavior for describe-only responses with more_rows=true and retain DRVQA25.8 fail-closed error cleanup.

Guard tests

1. Terminal native-JSON get_one returns None, sends no DEFINE-FETCH, releases one cached cursor, and queues no close.
2. Describe-only more_rows=true initial JSON result still performs DEFINE-FETCH; malformed response retires exactly once.
3. Existing successful and failing SODA cursor lifecycle tests remain green.

Acceptance

Focused TTC guards, all SODA/oracledb lib tests, live_soda compile, production clippy, fmt/diff, UBS and fresh-eyes compliance pass.
notes: VERIFIED TRUE AND FIXED. Pre-fix real TTC native-JSON get_one received a DESCRIBE plus terminal ORA-01403, returned None, but still sent a second DEFINE-FETCH; the server recorded it and the no-extra-round-trip assertion failed. Fix adds the protocol-terminal more_rows=false condition to execute_collect_with_binds before the complex-column define branch. The existing describe-only more_rows=true malformed DEFINE-FETCH guard still executes and retires cursor 91 exactly once. Terminal-empty guard now sends one execute only, returns None, releases cursor 128 to its one cached entry, and queues no close. Final shared-snapshot gates after this fix: cargo test -p oracledb --features soda --lib 318/318; production clippy -D warnings; live_soda target no-run; fmt and diff checks. Prior targeted UBS scan covers the changed helper file; cargo check/test build/clippy/audit/deny green and no actionable security finding. Awaiting independent fresh-eyes verdict; do not close or commit per root instruction.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: HANDOFF TOUCHED-HUNK MAP. Production: crates/oracledb/src/soda/collection.rs execute_collect_with_binds early terminal condition using result.more_rows. Guard: collection::tests::terminal_empty_native_json_query_skips_define_fetch. Test fixtures adjusted only to provide terminal/nonterminal json_describe_execute_response and native_json_collection. Counter-guard from .8: failed_initial_define_fetch_retires_cursor_once proves nonterminal describe-only results still DEFINE-FETCH.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh compliance PASS: terminal more_rows=false complex-column results return before DEFINE-FETCH, while the more_rows=true counter-guard still performs and fail-closes the define operation. The regression drives real native-JSON TTC and observes exactly one request. No blocker.

title: [DRVQA25][P1][SODA][SECURITY] Reject invalid native RAW identifiers
description:
Finding

Native embedded-OID keys and native ETAG versions are converted from public hexadecimal strings by operation::hex_decode. The decoder silently skips every invalid byte pair and drops an odd trailing nibble. Thus malformed identifiers are normalized into different valid RAW values: AAzzBB and AABB both bind bytes AA BB. The same helper feeds get/find/count/remove predicates and replace_one key/version predicates.

Independent reproduction

A builder-level regression first proves valid key AABB binds RAW [AA, BB], then requires AAzzBB and odd-length AAB to fail before SQL execution. On unchanged code AAzzBB succeeds and produces the identical RAW bind, failing the test. This is the exact value Oracle compares, not a display-only transformation.

False-positive check

Although returned document keys originate from RAWTOHEX, every key and version filter is public caller input and can be mistyped or attacker-controlled. Skipping invalid pairs is not permissive server validation: it changes identity. A valid A-F/a-f case difference is harmless and must remain accepted; non-hex or odd-length input must not alias another identifier.

Impact

A malformed key can read, count, replace, or delete the document addressed by its normalized bytes. In particular remove and replace_one can mutate the wrong document while appearing to target a syntactically different key. Malformed native version strings can likewise satisfy optimistic-lock predicates for another ETAG.

Proposed fix

Make RAW identifier decoding strict and fallible: require an even number of ASCII hex digits and reject the first invalid pair. Add a domain InvalidArgument error rather than misclassifying caller input as metadata, QBE, unsupported functionality, or a server failure. Propagate validation through single-key, multi-key, version, and replace builders before any wire operation.

Guard tests

1. AABB and mixed-case valid hex decode exactly; AAzzBB, AAB, and non-ASCII fail.
2. Single-key and multi-key select/remove builders reject any invalid native key.
3. Native version predicates reject invalid hex.
4. replace_one SQL construction rejects invalid RAW key and version.
5. Legacy VARCHAR2 keys remain opaque strings and unchanged.

Acceptance

Focused builder guards, all SODA/oracledb lib tests, live_soda compile, production clippy, fmt/diff, UBS security review and independent compliance pass.
notes: VERIFIED TRUE AND FIXED. Pre-fix builder proof showed public native key AAzzBB succeeded and produced exactly the same RAW [AA, BB] bind as valid key AABB; the required error assertion failed on unchanged code. This identity-changing normalization reached select/count/remove and replace key/version paths. Fix adds SodaError::InvalidArgument, makes hex_decode strictly fallible for odd length, non-ASCII, and any non-hex nibble, and propagates validation through single key, key lists, native ETAG predicates, and replace construction before wire I/O. Valid mixed-case hex remains accepted; legacy VARCHAR2 keys remain opaque. Passing guards cover valid/malformed decoder inputs, alias regression, key-list failure, native delete/version failure, and replace invalid key/version. Final gates: cargo test -p oracledb --features soda --lib 322/322; production clippy -D warnings; live_soda no-run; fmt/diff. UBS operation/error scan: cargo check, test build, fmt, clippy, audit, deny green. UBS labels the function name hex_decode as JWT bypass, a lexical false positive with no JWT/auth code; indexed pair access is proven length 2 by chunks_exact; SQL warning is existing parameterized builder structure. No actionable UBS security finding remains. Awaiting independent fresh-eyes verdict; do not close or commit per root instruction.

Guards: operation::tests::invalid_native_hex_cannot_alias_a_valid_raw_key; raw_identifier_decoder_is_strict_and_case_insensitive; every_native_filter_identifier_is_validated; collection::tests::replace_rejects_invalid_native_raw_key_and_version. The later .13 build_where movement is a separate hunk.

comments.text: Fresh compliance PASS after security call-site audit: every native single key, key list, version, delete and replace bind uses strict fallible RAW decoding; odd, empty, non-ASCII and non-hex inputs fail before wire I/O; mixed-case valid hex and legacy text keys remain unchanged. No alias path remains.

title: [DRVQA25][P0][SODA][DATA LOSS] Never drop a requested version predicate
description:
Finding

SodaOperation::build_where appends version_predicate only inside the single-key branch. A version supplied with a QBE filter, multiple keys, or no other selector is silently ignored. The same builder drives reads, counts, and DELETE. Most dangerously, SodaOperation { version: Some(v), ..default() } builds DELETE ... WHERE 1=1, so remove can delete the whole collection despite the caller requesting one version.

Independent reproduction

A builder regression requests filter age>18 plus version-1 and requires both JSON_EXISTS and VERSION = :1. Unchanged code emits only JSON_EXISTS with no bind. A second version-only case requires VERSION = :1 and no 1=1; unchanged code emits 1=1. This is the exact DELETE SQL used by SodaCollection::remove.

False-positive check

Oracle SODA documentation defines version() as finding only documents with the supplied version and explicitly permits using it with a filter that identifies at most one document. See https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/simple-oracle-document-access/java/adsda/using-soda-java.html#GUID-19373A12-DC81-427C-A942-07E378ABAE24 . Silently widening a predicate is not a compatibility choice. Applying version with any selector is fail-closed and consistent with the API field.

Impact

Optimistic-lock reads can return the wrong version. Filtered or multi-key remove can delete stale/nonmatching documents. A version-only remove widens to the entire collection, creating a direct data-loss path.

Proposed fix

Build the primary key/keys/filter clause first, then append version_predicate whenever version is Some, regardless of primary selector. Preserve bind ordering after any existing key binds and return the existing no-version-column error instead of ignoring caller intent.

Guard tests

1. key+version remains two predicates and two ordered binds.
2. filter+version emits both predicates with the version bind.
3. keys+version emits IN binds followed by the version bind.
4. version-only emits only the version predicate, never 1=1.
5. any requested version on metadata without a version column fails closed.
6. native RAW versions continue strict hexadecimal validation.

Acceptance

Focused builder guards, all SODA/oracledb lib tests, live_soda compile, clippy, fmt/diff, UBS and fresh-eyes compliance pass.
notes: VERIFIED TRUE AND FIXED. Pre-fix focused guard version_predicate_is_never_silently_dropped failed on unchanged code: filter plus version emitted DELETE WHERE JSON_EXISTS only with zero binds; version-only emitted WHERE 1=1. Root cause: build_where appended version_predicate only inside the single-key branch, so filter, keys, and no-primary-selector paths silently widened caller intent. Minimal fail-closed fix moves one version append after primary selector construction. Guards now prove exact bind order for key+version (:1 key, :2 version), filter+version (:1 version), keys+version (:1/:2 keys, :3 version), version-only without 1=1, requested version against metadata without a version column fails NotSupported, and native filter+invalid RAW version fails InvalidArgument. Focused version_predicate tests pass 2/2. Final shared-snapshot gates: cargo test -p oracledb --features soda --lib 323/323; cargo clippy -p oracledb --features soda --lib -- -D warnings; live_soda no-run; cargo fmt check; git diff check. Targeted UBS operation scan: cargo check/test build/fmt/clippy/audit/deny green. JWT alerts are lexical false positives on hex_decode; SQL warning is the existing parameterized builder. Touched hunk: crates/oracledb/src/soda/operation.rs build_where and tests version_predicate_on_key/version_predicate_is_never_silently_dropped. No other production function changed for .13. Freeze restored; no commit or close per root handoff.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: HANDOFF TOUCHED-HUNK MAP. Production: crates/oracledb/src/soda/operation.rs build_where removes version append from the key arm and appends version_predicate once after key/keys/filter selection. Guards: operation::tests::version_predicate_on_key and version_predicate_is_never_silently_dropped, covering key/filter/keys/version-only/no-version-column/native-invalid-version cases and exact bind order. No other production function belongs to .13.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Root fresh-eyes found and fixed one residual optimistic-lock widening path: build_replace_sql had its own version handling and silently ignored op.version when metadata lacked a version column. It now returns the same fail-closed NotSupported error as read/count/delete builders, with replace_never_ignores_a_requested_version guard. Combined full SODA lib is 324/324 and clippy/fmt/diff pass.
