title: [W3-E3] DPOR over the wire/cancel paths (asupersync lab)
description:
WHY: asupersync's LabRuntime+DPOR reaches a concurrency/cancel bug class the single-threaded python suite cannot. Runs on W1-T1's scripted transport + W1-T2's state machine (no separate seam needed).

VERIFIED 0.3.4 API (skill docs describe a newer tree): LabRuntime/LabConfig (asupersync src/lib.rs:402), DporExplorer/ScheduleExplorer/ExplorationReport (src/lab/explorer.rs), CoverageMetrics::is_saturated/discovery_rate (:165/156); test_utils::run_test is NOT the lab.

PATHS (verified crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs): P1 timeout->BREAK->drain->reuse (:6077/6234); P2 cancel/drop-cancel auto-drain (:6198/3003); P3 shared write-mutex vs CancelHandle (:5922 — REQUIRES the async CancelHandle::cancel(&Cx) variant built in W1-T2.4); P4 close-cursors piggyback vs in_use_cursors (:1246/1255, ScheduleExplorer); P5 speculative prefetch drop (:3171).

STOPPING: P1/P2/P4/P5 exhaustive (queue empties under recorded max_runs, 0 violations); P3 bounded (is_saturated(512) && discovery_rate<0.005 — a HEURISTIC, not an exhaustiveness proof; asupersync's documented guarantee is Optimal-DPOR EFFICIENCY, not saturation=exhaustiveness).

ORACLES AS HARD GATES (verified 0.3.4): set LabConfig panic_on_futurelock + panic_on_leak + futurelock_max_idle_steps (lab/config.rs) plus the QUIESCENCE oracle (lab/crashpack/oracle.rs), so a futurelock / obligation-leak / non-quiescent-shutdown violation FAILS THE RUN, not just a summary line; and test-assert the W1-T2 ready-or-dead contract + loser-drain (cancelled losers drain their cursor; cf. lab/atp_path/harness.rs). On any failure emit a crashpack (CrashPack, lab/crashpack/...) + repro_manifest (lab/replay.rs) + write_json_summary() (lab/explorer.rs:335) + reproducer seed (one-command replay). Layer deterministic chaos onto the cancel paths — with_light_chaos() in canary / with_heavy_chaos() in soak (lab/chaos.rs:49/53) over a VirtualTcpStream/VirtualNet surface (net/tcp/virtual_tcp.rs, lab/network/harness.rs).

ACCEPTANCE: all paths clean under recorded bounds; oracles green as gates; crashpack + seed captured on any failure.
TESTS: this task IS tests (the DPOR model).
DEPS: W1-T2 (state machine; subsumes W1-T1) + W1-T2.4 (async cancel handle for P3). Feeds W3-E8.
close_reason: DPOR wire/cancel: in-crate harness over scripted-transport seam; bounded operation-vs-interrupt exploration replaying real ConnectionCore wire recovery for each seed (full-ConnectionCore-in-LabRuntime fallback, documented). Verified UserCancel maps to Error::Cancelled, Timeout to CallTimeout, recovery ends Ready, exactly one BREAK + one RESET. Guards the W1-T6.2 cancel-checkpoint anti-pattern.

title: [W3-E4] DPOR over the async pool (shares the E3 lab; loom dropped)
description:
WHY: With W1-T7 the pool is ASYNC-NATIVE on asupersync sync primitives, so its interleavings are visible to the SAME DPOR/LabRuntime machinery as the wire/cancel paths (E3) — and LOOM IS REMOVED from the plan. Rationale: loom intercepts std/loom sync + atomics, whereas DPOR explores async task/await/obligation interleavings; once the pool holds no std::sync/atomics concurrency island, loom has nothing left to model. Guard: if any std-sync island DOES remain after W1-T7, loom covers ONLY that, reported bounded clean; otherwise loom is not a dependency.

SCOPE: model the W1-T7 pure PoolState shell + yielding waiter queue with a mock PoolBackend (pool.rs:90-109) under DPOR. INVARIANTS: no double-handout, no lost wakeup, no leaked waiter, no returned-retired connection, idempotent close, AND acquire-cancel returns (never leaks/duplicates) its connection (W1-T7). STOPPING: finite queue empties under recorded max_runs → exhaustive; else bounded clean (is_saturated/discovery_rate is a heuristic, not a completeness proof — §7). Oracles as hard gates + crashpack / repro_manifest / write_json_summary() + reproducer seed on any failure, exactly as E3.

ACCEPTANCE: all invariants hold under recorded bounds; oracles green as gates; artifacts captured on any failure.
TESTS: this task IS tests (the pool DPOR model).
DEPS: W1-T7. Feeds W3-E8.
close_reason: DPOR pool: exhaustive depth-7 lifecycle enumeration (3125 sequences) + async DPOR fallback. Found and FIXED a real bug (pool.rs:467): force-close racing in-flight open/ping failure requeued a waiter into a closed pool. Fix suppresses waiter requeue while closing. Invariants verified: no missed wakeup, FIFO, no double-handout, force-close drains waiters. Gates + conformance (0 regressions) green.

title: [W3-E5] Deterministic fault & fragmentation matrix
description:
WHY: On W1-T1's scripted transport, prove the decoder is invariant to BYTE SPLITS and robust to faults at every protocol phase — without a live DB. This is the metamorphic backbone of the sans-io layer.

SCOPE: generate fault points from protocol phases (connect/auth/execute/fetch/LOB/cancel/close) and inject short read/write, Pending, EOF, I/O error, timeout, virtual-time advances; assert FRAGMENTATION INVARIANCE (every legal byte split → same semantic result); exercise faults around BREAK/RESET (next op never consumes the previous response). Run the fragmentation-invariance property against the PURE decoder (proptest over arbitrary split points), not only through the async transport, so the sans-io layer carries its own metamorphic guarantee independent of any runtime.

ACCEPTANCE: a generated phase/fault coverage report; every case ends Ready/Dead/Closed; no live DB.
TESTS: this task IS tests (the fault/fragmentation matrix + the pure-decoder split-invariance property).
DEPS: W2-T1 (stabilized types). Feeds W3-E8.
close_reason: E5 deterministic fault and fragmentation matrix complete: fragmentation_invariance.rs (split-invariance, committed 600ec18) + fault_injection.rs (fault-point generator, all phases). Decoders proven robust under corruption; no bug found.

title: [W3-E6] Secure full-connection cassette replay
description:
WHY: Seam already built (format net/cassette.rs; Recording/Replay transport transport.rs:49-71; capture scope; committed fixture; offline replay test). The REAL work is replaying the WHOLE ConnectionCore offline + making fixtures security-safe. (Overlaps the pre-existing bead rust-oracledb-1s2 — this is the Road-to-1.0 spec of that intent.)

SCOPE:
1. Replay the whole ConnectionCore through the W1-T1 ReplayTransport (prod + replay share framing/state — no public seam).
2. Fixtures are SECURITY-SENSITIVE: a manifest (format/schema ver, source commit, server profile, charset/TZ, scenario, sanitizer ver, checksum, expected-writes, sanitized:true); disposable accounts OR synthetic post-auth transcripts; a scrubber + leak scanner; NEVER commit production captures.
3. Strict replay fails on unexpected writes / unread frames / checksum mismatch / truncation / unsupported version.
4. Enable --features cassette in CI (zero refs today).

ACCEPTANCE: strict offline full-ConnectionCore replay in CI; sanitized fixtures with provenance; an altered decoder fails the replay.
TESTS: the replay test itself + a deliberately-altered-decoder negative test + a leak-scanner test over fixtures.
DEPS: W2-T1 (stabilized core). Feeds W3-E8. Related: rust-oracledb-1s2.
close_reason: E6 epic complete: secure full-connection cassette replay E6.1 E6.2 E6.3; 12 cassette tests green

TESTS: every confirmed finding lands a regression test before it is closed.
DEPS: W3-E1, E2, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7, E9 (consumes their findings). Feeds W4-T1.
close_reason: E8 SUBSTANTIALLY CONVERGED. 5 exhaustive adversarial rounds (3 Codex passes x2 + Workflow 10-finder/3-skeptic x3) + R6 (rate-limited) + 2 clean orchestrator self-audits (LOB offset, datetime TZ). ~25 real correctness bugs found+fixed across protocol/codec/multi-packet/async + driver API + pyshim (commits 787d2d4, 9ffc785, bb5187b, 9e5e6d4, 93935d3): incl data corruption (long-bind ordering), pool livelock, credential leak (ConnectOptions Debug), CLOB/BLOB+UROWID decode desyncs, DbObject format, query_one/LONG, execute_many RETURNING, f32/interval/NUMBER-JSON precision, AUTH serial. Layered review caught 2 of the agents' own bad/incomplete fixes. Conformance 2577 pass, 0 code regressions (1 env diff = orphan CQN reg n5s2). All findings resolved or tracked exceptions: clvm (transport gaps), x2ye (into_typed W4-T1), n5s2 (orphan reg), deferred scroll. title: [WAVE-4] Freeze & release — RC, exact-SHA qualification, provenance preflight, publish 1.0.0
description:
Wave 4 freezes and ships. Tasks: W4-T1 cut 1.0.0-rc.1 (remove pre-1.0 #[deprecated] shims + accidental public internals; freeze the intended 1.x surface); W4-T2 qualify the exact RC SHA via the release-qualification workflow at soak-equivalent budget (every gate + live/oraclemcp suites + perf comparison on the FROZEN RC commit — the only definitive 1.0 gate; any code change → a new RC); W4-T3 packaged-source & provenance preflight (close the inter-crate version-pin gap that bit 0.2.1/0.2.2; build/test .crate artifacts standalone; SBOM + build attestations); W4-T4 publish 1.0.0 in dependency order + tag + build a clean consumer + oraclemcp from the public registry; the cargo public-api snapshot becomes the 1.x baseline.

title: [W4-T4] Publish 1.0.0
description: PLAN CHANGED 2026-06-23: instead of publishing 1.0.0 directly, the qualified 1.x-contract code ships as 0.5.0 to crates.io first for real-world production validation, then promotes to 1.0.0 when the feeling is right. The exact-SHA qualification per ADR-0003 was green for this code: CI release-qualification all checks PASS incl fuzz; python-oracledb thin differential 2578=2578 with 0 regressions; local gates fmt/clippy/test/semver/preflight/package green; live driver suite green. Promotion to 1.0.0 [this bead, kept OPEN] now requires BOTH: 1] oraclemcp production-validation of 0.5.0 reports clean [blocked-by rust-oracledb-vm2f], AND 2] a fresh exact-SHA re-qualification at the 1.0 cut, because any code change after the published 0.5.0 SHA is a new candidate per ADR-0003. Remaining for the 1.0 publish itself: bump to 1.0.0, tag, publish_crates.sh in dep order, build a clean consumer from the registry, set the 1.x public-api baseline. DEFERRED until the operator says the feeling is right.

title: Agent skill for the oracledb crate + oraclemcp-style CI/release devops
close_reason: Added .claude/skills/oracledb (SKILL.md + API.md + INTEGRATION.md), verified against source by self-review + a read-only codex review (11 inaccuracies found & fixed: ClientIdentity arg order, cell() -> Option<&QueryValue>, BlockingConnection static-shim connect/close shapes, async-only fetch_ref/notify/recv, feature defaults wording, forbid(unsafe_code) scoped to published crates, r2d2 pools Connection not BlockingConnection, blocking executemany variants). Added .github/workflows/ci.yml (fmt/clippy/test/msrv-1.83/docs/static-musl/cargo-deny; pyshim excluded - needs Python) and release.yml (tag-driven GitHub Release + credential-safe guarded crates.io publish: token only as repo secret, no-op until set, pre-releases skipped, dep-order publish). Tag-driven publish structurally guarantees GitHub is in sync before crates.io.

title: Revisit rsa dep / RUSTSEC-2023-0071 when a constant-time RSA release ships
description: OCI IAM request-signing (bead cco) added the RustCrypto 'rsa' 0.9 crate, which carries RUSTSEC-2023-0071 (Marvin timing side-channel) with no fixed version available. A documented deny.toml ignore was added: the advisory targets an RSA DECRYPTION oracle (attacker submits chosen ciphertexts, observes timing); our usage is a one-shot client-side RSA PKCS1v15 SHA-256 SIGNATURE at connect time with no attacker-queryable decryption oracle, so it is not practically exploitable here. python-oracledb gets constant-time RSA from OpenSSL/cryptography; our pure-Rust + forbid-unsafe constraint has no constant-time alternative today. ACTION: when RustCrypto ships a constant-time 'rsa' release, upgrade and remove the deny.toml ignore. RELEASE GATE: this dep + advisory-ignore must be an EXPLICIT, operator-acknowledged decision before the next crates.io release — do not publish silently.
notes: RESOLVED BY REMOVAL 2026-07-04: operator rejected the rsa dep; IAM signing + rsa + deny-ignore removed. This bead now tracks: only re-introduce RSA (for IAM signing) if/when RustCrypto ships a constant-time release.

title: Statement-suite ground truth differential: rust vs python-oracledb field-by-field over identical corpus (xe18 + free23)
description: Ground-truth differential harness (Mission 2, 2026-07-04): an IDENTICAL statement corpus is executed by BOTH the Rust driver (crates/oracledb/examples/statement_ground_truth.rs, canonical-JSON emitter) and python-oracledb 4.0.1 (scripts/statement_ground_truth.py, .venv-py313) against a live lane; outputs are diffed field-by-field (numbers as exact Decimal, floats as IEEE bit patterns, LOBs as length+checksum, errors as first ORA-NNNNN code). Corpus: NUMBER edge values (1e125/1e-130/38-digit/negative-scale), VARCHAR2/NVARCHAR2/unicode, empty-string-is-NULL, DATE/TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMP TZ (session TZ pinned +00:00), RAW, BINARY_FLOAT/BINARY_DOUBLE incl. +/-inf, FLOAT(126), bind round-trips, PL/SQL OUT binds, DML rows_affected, CLOB/BLOB roundtrip, fetch page boundaries (99/100/101/250 rows), error surfaces (ORA-00942/00904/01476/00900). Wired as (a) env-gated live test crates/oracledb/tests/statement_ground_truth.rs (ignored unless PYO_TEST_* set; runs rust in-process, python twin via subprocess, diffs) and (b) scripts/version_matrix.sh truth subcommand per lane (xe18 AND free23 required by the mission). Any field mismatch = a bug: file a discovered-from bead per real mismatch and fix clear-cut ones.
close_reason: Landed: examples/statement_ground_truth.rs + python twin + version_matrix.sh truth subcommand run an identical 29-case corpus (scalar types incl NUMBER edges, NULLs, binds, PL/SQL OUT, DML rows_affected, LOBs, page boundaries, error surfaces) through rust AND python-oracledb 4.0.1 and diff field-by-field. RESULT: IDENTICAL on xe18 and free23. Found + fixed the TSTZ instant bug (97cj) and the stale-bind-metadata cache bug beaded as ilel.

title: perf: borrowed fast-path only snapshots last row (stop materializing every cell to owned) lib.rs:5739
description: DEFERRED (reverted, kept correct-by-reasoning). The borrowed for_each_row_ref paging loop (lib.rs ~5743) materializes EVERY row to owned just to seed the next page's duplicate-column resolution; only the LAST row is ever used as the seed. A row_count()-gated 'materialize only the last row' is correct by reasoning [row_count()==row_starts.len()==the for_each iteration count, so last_owned == the last row, identical seed]. BLOCKER: the only live test exercising the exact concern [cross-page bit-vector seed] is borrowed_fetch_handles_multipage_clob_lob_prefetch, which is #[ignore] AND currently environment-flaky [fails on clean HEAD too — see the new sibling bead]. Per no-unverified-changes, NOT shipped until a DETERMINISTIC regression exists [e.g. a non-LOB query with a constant column forcing bit-vector duplication across a small arraysize, run via for_each_row_ref] AND a clean container. perf2+perf3 shipped separately.
close_reason: RE-APPLIED + VERIFIED now that bur7 unblocked the CLOB seed test. for_each_row_ref now snapshots ONLY the last row of each page as the next-page duplicate-column seed [row_count()==row_starts.len()==the for_each iteration count, so last_owned is the same last row the old overwrite-every-row logic produced], instead of materializing every borrowed row to owned. Verified: live_borrowed_fetch 2/2 across 3 deterministic runs [the duplicate-CLOB compression test is the exact seed verifier — it exercises cross-page bit-vector resolution]; clippy/fmt/gen_baseline[public_api unchanged] green. The stranded_prefetch test failure is a SEPARATE pre-existing #[ignore] issue [fails without perf1 too; does not use for_each_row_ref] tracked separately.

title: PERF: fuse NUMBER digit walk — accumulate i128 coefficient inline in decode_number_parts_stack, drop the second digits_to_i128 pass (byte-identical OracleNumber gated)
close_reason: NUMBER digit-fusion merged (perf-decode): +14-38% decode, byte-identical

title: [CONF-GAP] Close 103 pre-existing python-oracledb conformance gaps (4 root causes)
description:
DISCOVERED via the first CLEAN full differential conformance run (2026-06-21, 2578/2578 tests, env fixed). 103 tests pass in python-oracledb (baseline) but fail in our driver. PROVEN PRE-EXISTING / NOT a Wave-2 regression: validate_bind_rows_shape + the dataframe/Arrow + protocol paths are git-unchanged by Wave-2; old methods and the new execute_raw both funnel through the SAME execute_query_with_bind_rows_and_options_core (lib.rs:5196), so the migration is conformance-neutral (stash/static-proven). These are correctness-to-convergence gaps to close before 1.0.

ROOT CAUSES (counts from the clean run):
 1) 66 bind-shape validation too strict (crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs validate_bind_rows_shape, gated only by !scroll_operation at lib.rs:5195):
    - 14 parse-only/no-binds: cursor.parse(sql) with placeholders + 0 binds. python-oracledb parse() DESCRIBES without binding; our core validates bind count on parse_only -> rejects. FIX CANDIDATE: also skip validation when exec_options.parse_only() (mirror the scroll_operation skip). e.g. test_5416, test_4311, test_4324, test_1614, test_6308.
    - 52 partial-bind mismatch: execute with fewer binds than placeholders (e.g. 'expects 3 but 1 supplied'). python-oracledb defers the bind-count error to the SERVER (ORA-01008) or allows it; our client-side validation rejects with a different message. e.g. test_8616, test_3916, test_4216.
 2) 24 'protocol resource limit exceeded' (oracledb.exceptions.DatabaseError) in the DataFrame/Arrow fetch path (fetch_df_all / fetch_df_batches). e.g. test_9700-range, test_8048/9324 families. Investigate the resource-limit/protocol cap in the Arrow/dataframe fetch.
 3) 12 error-code mismatch: driver raises a different DPY-/ORA- code than the reference expects. e.g. test_9714.
 4) 1 expected-error-not-raised: test_5528 expects DPY-4005 (pool) not raised.

ACCEPTANCE: harness/run.sh diff shows 0 regressions (or each remaining one is explicitly justified + filtered with rationale). Add unit/e2e coverage for each fix. Gates green.

SUGGEST: granularize into 4 child beads (one per root cause) when scheduled. This BLOCKS a clean 1.0 (W4-T2 RC qualification needs 0-regression conformance). DEPS: discovered-from W2-T1.2 conformance run.
notes: ALL 3 ROOT CAUSES FIXED (pending final full-conformance confirmation): bind-validation 66 (validate_bind_rows_shape rectangular-only), direct-path-load 36 (removed misused check_batch_rows(batch_size)), pool DPY-4005 1 (asupersync wheel-timer bug — short timed-wait timer fires only on 1st use/process; fixed via TimedAcquireDeadline std-thread watchdog in pool.rs + pool teardown GIL-deadlock fixed via helper-thread runtime drop). Pool fix: diagnosed by Claude teammate (deterministic repro), implemented+verified by Codex (test_5528 x5 + module 47 + test_2400 51 + gates green), adversarially reviewed by me (sound: watchdog gated on TIMEDWAIT, no leak, EDEADLK preserved, connections still closed sync). Full conformance running for 0-regression confirmation.
close_reason: Done: all 103 differential-conformance gaps closed — full suite 2578/2578, 0 regressions. bind-validation 66 + direct-path-load 36 + pool DPY-4005 1; triangulated (Claude+Codex) + verified.

title: SODA unusable on Oracle 18c (driver SODA path needs JSON_SERIALIZE, 21c+)
description: Version-suite proof: live_soda 5/5 FAIL on xe18 (18c) with ORA-00904 'JSON_SERIALIZE: invalid identifier' at collection create; JSON_SERIALIZE is a 21c+ SQL function, and USER_SODA_COLLECTIONS view is absent on this 18c (verified: select count(*) from all_views = 0). GREEN on xe21 (21c) and free23. This is a genuine server-feature boundary: the driver's SODA implementation depends on 21c+ SQL. GATE live_soda on <21c lanes WITH this proof in docs/VERSION_MATRIX.md (never a silent skip). Full pre-21c SODA support (older SODA SQL path, as python-oracledb does) is a real feature gap tracked here — NOT deferred silently, explicitly bounded with evidence.

title: [bug] fetch_rows_ref does not arm BREAK-recovery on cancel/drop (borrowed fetch desync)

A dropped borrowed-fetch strands the response -> next op desyncs. Fix: mirror fetch_rows_with_columns (ensure_clean + read_response_cancellable) + ScriptedTransport regression.
close_reason: FIXED. fetch_rows_ref mirrors fetch_rows_with_columns (ensure_clean_before_request + read_response_cancellable); drop-mid-read ScriptedTransport regression arms BreakSent.

title: [test-gap] 0.5.1: regression-test 3 shipped typed-diagnostic branches (p12/encrypted-PEM/Kerberos-Radius pre-IO)
description:
Origin: 2026-06-29 fresh-eyes compliance verification (/beads-compliance-and-completion-verification) of the 5 CLOSED 0.5.1 beads (o0b, qm4, x1p, doc-truth-051, sqp2). Result: ALL FIVE verified SATISFIED / correctly closed (re-ran tests + cargo doc; not false-closed). HOWEVER three typed-diagnostic code branches that shipped in 0.5.1 are reachable but NOT regression-tested. These are precisely the typed surfaces oraclemcp depends on for `oraclemcp doctor`, so guard them before 0.5.1 publishes. ALL additive tests -- NO production-code change. This bead is self-contained; do not consult other docs.

GAP 1 (from x1p) -- ewallet.p12 -> WalletError::UnsupportedFormat untested.
  Branch: crates/oracledb/src/tls.rs:372-377 returns WalletError::UnsupportedFormat { format: "ewallet.p12" } when load_wallet sees a dir whose only wallet file is ewallet.p12 (it tries pem, then sso, then p12). Type defined at crates/oracledb-protocol/src/tls/wallet.rs:60-61.
  TEST: create a temp dir containing ONLY an ewallet.p12 file (empty/dummy bytes are fine -- the branch keys off existence), call the wallet loader, assert Err is WalletError::UnsupportedFormat with format == "ewallet.p12", and assert neither Display ({err}) nor Debug ({err:?}) contains the temp dir path (redaction). Mirror the redaction style of crates/oracledb-protocol/src/tls/wallet.rs:317 wallet_errors_redact_paths_in_display_and_debug.

GAP 2 (from x1p) -- encrypted ewallet.pem -> WalletError::Pem typed error untested.
  Branch: crates/oracledb-protocol/src/tls/wallet.rs:222-228 returns WalletError::Pem("wallet private key is encrypted; supply ... unencrypted ewallet.pem (orapki ... -auto_login) or use cwallet.sso") when pem_contains_encrypted_key (wallet.rs:245-258) detects an encrypted PKCS#8 marker and no usable key was parsed.
  TEST: build (or fixture) an ewallet.pem whose private key is an ENCRYPTED PKCS#8 block (BEGIN ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY), call parse_ewallet_pem, assert Err is WalletError::Pem, assert the message mentions the orapki/-auto_login remediation, and assert no filesystem path leaks in Display/Debug. (This guards the typed-diagnostic; it does NOT add decryption -- decryption stays deferred in rust-oracledb-encrypted-pem-p12-wallets-1u8f.)

GAP 3 (from qm4) -- unsupported-AuthMode pre-network rejection only dedicated-tested for External.
  Branch: the guard at crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs:2142-2144 returns Err(Error::UnsupportedAuthMode) BEFORE EasyConnect::parse (lib.rs:2145) and the first TcpStream::connect_timeout (lib.rs:2170). Existing test crates/oracledb/tests/access_token.rs:100-109 covers External only; Kerberos/Radius ride the identical shared guard but have no dedicated connect-time assertion.
  TEST: extend access_token.rs (or add cases) asserting that connect() with AuthMode::Kerberos{..} and AuthMode::Radius{..} returns Error::UnsupportedAuthMode with reason.mode() == AuthModeKind::Kerberos / ::Radius respectively, and that it fails WITHOUT opening a socket (e.g. point at an unroutable/closed host:port and assert the error is UnsupportedAuthMode, not a connect/timeout error -- proving the pre-I/O early return). Keep keytab/principal/challenge redaction assertions.

ACCEPTANCE: 3 new tests added and GREEN (cargo test --workspace); each asserts the correct typed error variant; the two wallet tests additionally assert zero path/password leakage in Display+Debug; the qm4 test proves rejection happens before any socket I/O; NO production code changed (if a branch turns out unreachable as written, fix the test, not the code, and note why). Run before the 0.5.1 publish.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for p12-only wallet UnsupportedFormat redaction, encrypted ewallet.pem typed remediation/redaction, and Kerberos/Radius pre-network UnsupportedAuthMode rejection; gates green.

title: Implement real async split transport cancellation handle
description: After replacing std::net::TcpStream with asupersync::net::TcpStream, the driver no longer has a same-socket clone for CancelHandle::cancel(). Python conn.cancel() currently uses the shim cancellation flag and tested behavior passes, but the public Rust CancelHandle now fail-closes. Implement a real split/owned-half or equivalent same-connection break-marker design without reintroducing blocking socket clones.
close_reason: Implemented split asupersync transport write half for real TNS break-marker cancellation; added cancel response drain; verified cargo tests and test_1100_connection.py 56 passed / 6 skipped

title: Move remaining async shim operations off BlockingConnection
description: Async execute/fetch now use direct oracledb::Connection futures and pass test_5300/test_5400. Remaining async shim paths still route through BlockingConnection or sync delegates: AsyncThinCursorImpl.executemany, AsyncThinLob read/write/trim/free, AsyncThinConnImpl connect/close/ping/commit/rollback/get_type/create_temp_lob. test_6100_cursor_executemany_async currently reports 16 passed, 18 failed, 1 error; several failures are protocol/bind gaps, but this issue tracks removing the blocking shim path with a connection-owned async runner before claiming native async parity.

title: [bug] f32::from_sql overflows large NUMBER to infinity instead of OutOfRange
description: E8 Round-1 Pass A. impl FromSql for f32 = Ok(f64::from_sql? as f32); a finite f64 exceeding f32 range casts to inf silently (NUMBER 1e39 -> Ok(inf)), inconsistent with f64's non-finite rejection. Fix: is_finite check -> Err(OutOfRange). sql_convert.rs.
close_reason: FIXED. f32::from_sql now returns OutOfRange for finite f64 overflowing f32 (instead of inf); unit regression added. Non-breaking behavioral fix.

FIX: add BOOLEAN-negative-bytes->None branch at all 3 sites. Unit regression.

title: oraclemcp production-validation of 0.5.0 green-lights 1.0.0 promotion
description: The 1.x-contract code, exhaustively qualified, is shipped as 0.5.0 to crates.io for real-world validation BEFORE making the 1.0 stability promise. Gate: oraclemcp upgrades to oracledb 0.5.0 from the public registry, exercises it in production-representative use, and reports back. PASS criteria: oraclemcp builds + its suites pass against 0.5.0 from crates.io; no new driver defects surface in real use over a soak window. On PASS this becomes the production-readiness signal that, together with a fresh exact-SHA re-qualification at the 1.0 cut, green-lights the 1.0.0 promotion. Blocks rust-oracledb-road-to-1-0-llv.5.4.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED — the 0.5.0 production-validation gate is overtaken by events: the driver progressed 0.5->0.7->0.8, and oraclemcp 0.8.0 (published) validates oracledb 0.8.2 from crates.io in production with full green suites — far beyond the 0.5.0 soak this bead specified. The 1.0 stability promise is tracked separately in the ROAD-to-1.0 epic, not gated on 0.5.0.

title: Public structured ADT object/collection decoding API with metadata (native) (oraclemcp #9)
description: From oraclemcp GH issue #9. VERIFIED current state: QueryValue::Object(Box<ObjectValue>) carries the packed pickle image (types.rs:99,171); oracledb-protocol/src/thin/dbobject.rs has decode_dbobject_text / decode_dbobject_xmltype_text (a TEXT projection used by the parity suite). The codec exists internally. GAP: no PUBLIC API to decode QueryValue::Object / QueryValue::Array into STRUCTURED Rust values with metadata — object type schema+name, attribute names/types/nullability, scalar attributes decoded with the same rules as normal columns, collection element-type metadata, bounded nested traversal, and a lossless fallback for values it cannot decode. oraclemcp emits opaque/unsupported markers today so agents don't mistake packed protocol bytes for decoded business values. ACCEPTANCE: decode simple ADT objects with scalar attributes + collections with scalar elements; explicit machine-classifiable 'unsupported' for complex/nested shapes; enough metadata for agent-facing JSON to name fields+types; BOUNDED decode so malformed/huge object payloads cannot cause unbounded memory use; live tests for a simple object type and a simple collection type.
close_reason: Implemented public structured ADT decode: describe_object_type (ALL_TYPE_ATTRS for objects, ALL_COLL_TYPES for collections) + decode_object for scalar attributes and collection elements, bounded against the image, typed UnsupportedFeature for nested/unsupported shapes. Live tests for a simple object (incl. NULL attr) and a VARRAY of NUMBER (incl. NULL element + empty). Commit 85abc4c.

title: Wallet: ewallet.p12 (and evaluate cwallet.sso) support for TCPS/ADB
description:
Support cwallet.sso and standalone ewallet.p12 wallets for TCPS/mTLS connects (OCI Autonomous Database default wallet zips), or at minimum the p12 half.

Background: only unencrypted ewallet.pem works today; cwallet.sso and ewallet.p12 are detected and refused with structured diagnostics (good fail-closed posture, but blocks zero-config ADB). Field impact: ADB mTLS wallet zips ship cwallet.sso + ewallet.p12; users must hand-extract a PEM today. python-oracledb thin reads ewallet.p12 with the wallet password natively.

Plan: implement PKCS12 parsing first (pure Rust, license-clean, no C - e.g. a rustls-ecosystem pkcs12 crate vetted through deny.toml); evaluate cwallet.sso (Oracle obfuscated SSO container) separately and keep it fail-closed with a pointer to the p12/pem path if infeasible. All diagnostics keep the existing structured wallet-mode truth table shape.

Acceptance: live TCPS connect using an ADB-style wallet's ewallet.p12 + wallet password (lab TCPS listener or real ADB), docs updated, deny.toml clean.
close_reason: Duplicate of rust-oracledb-encrypted-pem-p12-wallets-1u8f (already scoped for 0.5.2); field context merged there

title: Columnar decode straight into Arrow builders (transpose-during-parse, no QueryValue String)
notes: Dueling-wizards consensus #1 (cross-model 820). Decode fetched rows directly into per-column typed accumulators (i128+scale Decimal128, offsets+bytes, NullBuffer) during parse, skipping QueryValue row materialization on the arrow path. Python-structurally-impossible (GIL per cell). Substrate exists: borrowed fetch (0ta), decode-offload (xad), arrow.rs. CONSTRAINT: wire is row-major (fetch.rs:667/693) so this is transpose-during-parse, NOT column-major wire read (effort L-XL). Gate behind `arrow` feature; differential proptest must match build_record_batch cell-for-cell. Pairs with the inline-i128-NUMBER bead.
close_reason: Columnar-Arrow merged (perf-push 1f4d6c2): 95% fewer allocs, byte-identical

close_reason: Native pipelining merged (perf-pipeline 86ff6a4): N->1 round trip, byte-identical

title: Cancellation-correct fetch (Scope + break marker): timeout() truly cancels, clean conn
close_reason: Delivered + merged: cancel-correct fetch / OTel spans; live tests + parity baseline

title: Broaden + document thin wallet compatibility: encrypted PEM, p12, typed format diagnostics (oraclemcp #6)
description: From oraclemcp GH issue #6. VERIFIED CURRENT STATE (so the bead is honest): we DO support — unencrypted ewallet.pem (oracledb-protocol/src/tls/wallet.rs parse_ewallet_pem), cwallet.sso (experimental feature, via the internal PKCS12 parser tls/pfx.rs used only by tls/sso.rs), a wallet password through ConnectOptions (wallet_password / with_wallet_password), AND a TYPED diagnostic when the PEM key is encrypted ('wallet private key is encrypted; supply an unencrypted ewallet.pem (orapki -auto_login) or use cwallet.sso', wallet.rs:194-197). So issue #6 UNDER-states current support. REAL GAPS: (a) encrypted ewallet.pem PKCS8 private keys are NOT decrypted even when wallet_password is supplied (we error) — add decryption; (b) no standalone ewallet.p12 wallet format (pfx parser is private/internal to cwallet.sso); (c) no operator-facing documentation of the supported-format matrix. ACCEPTANCE: encrypted-wallet handling where feasible; typed/machine-classifiable errors for unsupported formats; redact wallet paths+passwords in ALL formatted output (already partially done); tests for encrypted-wallet handling + unsupported-format diagnostics; docs/ section listing ewallet.pem (unencrypted), cwallet.sso, wallet_password usage, and what each error means.
acceptance_criteria: 0.5.1 deliverable (DOCS + DIAGNOSTICS ONLY): a docs/SUPPORT.md wallet-format matrix (ewallet.pem unencrypted [supported], encrypted ewallet.pem [typed error today], cwallet.sso [experimental feature], ewallet.p12 [unsupported today], wallet_password usage, required companion files) stating what each error means in operator terms; typed/machine-classifiable errors for unsupported formats; a full redaction sweep so wallet path + password never appear in any formatted or Debug output. NOTE current support already EXCEEDS what GH #6 states -- this release makes it legible, it does not add crypto. Encrypted-PEM PKCS8 decrypt + standalone p12 are split to the deferred follow-up bead (target 0.5.2) and are NOT in 0.5.1. Scoped into Release 0.5.1. GH #6.
notes: Encrypted-PEM/p12 impl split to rust-oracledb-encrypted-pem-p12-wallets-1u8f (target 0.5.2). 0.5.1 ships only docs/SUPPORT matrix + typed diagnostics + redaction.
close_reason: Updated docs/SUPPORT.md wallet matrix, added WalletError::UnsupportedFormat for ewallet.p12, preserved encrypted PEM as typed wallet error, and redacted wallet paths/passwords in Display/Debug and ConnectOptions Debug.

title: [security] ConnectOptions Debug leaks plaintext password and wallet_password
description: E8 R4 (2/3, security). ConnectOptions #[derive(Clone, Debug)] (lib.rs:2990) with password: String (2998) + wallet_password: Option<String> (3021) raw + NO custom Debug -> {:?} (tracing::debug!(?options), error logs) prints plaintext password + wallet password. access_token already redacted via AccessToken wrapper. FIX: custom Debug for ConnectOptions redacting password + wallet_password. Unit regression asserts no secret in {:?}.
close_reason: FIXED. Removed derive(Debug); custom Debug for ConnectOptions redacts password + wallet_password + access_token (***redacted***). Unit regression connect_options_debug_redacts_passwords asserts no secret in {:?}.

title: [bug] async Rows::into_typed silently discards unfetched batches (data loss)
description: E8 Round-1 Pass C. async Rows::into_typed(lib.rs:2533) release_cursor() then types only self.batch -> multi-batch result returns first batch only, discards rest; blocking into_typed(:2659) collect()s first. Fix: drain all batches before typing (likely async signature) + live regression.
close_reason: Fixed in 1.0.0-rc.1 [W4-T1]: async Rows::into_typed now async+cx and drains all batches via collect before typing; live regression async_rows_into_typed_drains_all_batches asserts 105 rows across 5 fetch batches. Blocking variant was already correct.

title: Async streaming row Stream / lending iterator over borrowed batches
notes: Dueling-wizards (both proposed independently, 730/660). Builds on for_each_row_ref + BorrowedRowBatch (lib.rs). HONEST framing: page-granular pull, not item-level backpressure (TTC pushes a configured array per round trip); API bifurcates into lending next_row (zero-copy) + an owned-row futures::Stream adapter. Constant-memory large-result fetch is the win. Effort L.
close_reason: VERIFIED DONE — shipped in oracledb 0.8.2. Async streaming row Stream = pub struct OwnedRowStream + Connection::into_row_stream/into_query_stream (futures_core::Stream, row_stream.rs:107/417). Borrowed lending path = Connection::for_each_row_ref (lib.rs:5471) + fetch_rows_ref. Both halves delivered.

title: Decode offload to asupersync worker pool: the GIL-killer (split socket-read from CPU decode)
notes: Framing (B) speculative next-page prefetch, one-page look-ahead. Chosen over (A) worker-pool decode-offload: decode stays on the SAME task so borrowed QueryValueRef buffers never cross a thread/await boundary (no unsafe, no lifetime risk); reuses the existing array-fetch round trip; no channel/worker. for_each_row_ref now sends page K+1 FETCH (fetch_rows_request, send-only) before decoding page K, reads K+1 next iter (fetch_rows_ref_response). Cancellation: fetch_rows_request arms cancel_drain_pending for the whole window until consumed; a drop anywhere -> next op break+drains the stranded page (merged CancelDrainGuard+break_and_drain). MEASURED loopback: read-wait/page -5.6%..-24% (robust overlap signal), realistic-consumer wall -12.5%..-19.5%, criterion end-to-end noise-to-modest on loopback (win larger on real-network RTT). SOUND: byte-identical rows, cancellation-safe (tested + negative-control proof). PARITY EXACT: 1100 57p/5s, 2200 39p, 4300 73p, 8000_dataframe 82p (pyshim untouched). Files: crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs, tests/prefetch_overlap.rs, benches/thin_driver.rs (oracledb_prefetch), examples/profile_fetch_attribution.rs, tests/artifacts/perf/xad-*. ALSO SATISFIES bead 3oi.
close_reason: Speculative next-page prefetch (framing B) implemented + proven: read-wait -5.6%..-24% (overlap), realistic-consumer wall -12.5%..-19.5%, sound (no unsafe, cancellation-safe w/ negative-control, byte-identical), parity exact (1100/2200/4300/8000), gate green. Also satisfies 3oi.

title: [bug] borrowed fetch path ignores LOB-prefetch decode mode (CLOB/BLOB desync on page 2+ via for_each_row_ref/arrow)
description: E8 R4 (3/3). Borrowed-path TWIN of the owned jbh9 fix. parse_query_response_borrowed* hardcode LobDecodeMode::PlainLocator (fetch.rs:1690/1369/1613); owned fetch_rows selects mode from lob_prefetch_cursors (lib.rs:5686). for_each_row_ref + arrow columnar paged fetch via fetch_rows_ref_response always PlainLocator -> for a LOB-prefetch cursor the size(ub8)+chunk_size(ub4) prefix is mis-framed on page 2+ -> corruption or invalid ub8 length. FIX: additive DefineMetadata borrowed parse variant + select from lob_prefetch_cursors like owned. Ref packet.pyx read_lob_with_length.
close_reason: FIXED. Additive parse_define_fetch_response_borrowed_with_limits + fetch_rows_ref/_response select DefineMetadata from lob_prefetch_cursors (matches owned lib.rs:5781). Borrowed multi-page CLOB/BLOB no longer desyncs on page 2+. Offline borrowed-vs-owned round-trip regression + ignored live. Semver additive, conformance 0 regressions.

title: Implement async thin shim bridge for AsyncThinConnImpl/AsyncThinCursorImpl
close_reason: Implemented AsyncThinConnImpl/AsyncThinCursorImpl bridge, deferred current_schema parity, async connect credential capture, minimal async pool close surface, and non-blocking async execute cancellation; test_5300_connection_async.py now passes 47/48 with 1 baseline skip.

title: Cross-version parity hardening: make version-gate bugs structurally unshippable
description: Strategy epic. Every cross-version bug this session (DPL ub8 token, TPC token, recovery framing, and the CQN/SODA boundaries) was a place where the reference (python-oracledb thin) gates the wire format on a negotiated capability/version and we either wrote a field UNCONDITIONALLY (missing gate) or had no test exercising the gated path. There are only ~56 such gate points in the entire reference thin protocol (grep _caps.ttc_field_version / _caps.supports_ / protocol_version>= / >= TNS_*VERSION*), concentrated in base.pyx(10) then subscribe/fast_auth/execute/connect/aq. That finite surface is the spine of a layered defense: L0 centralize the version surface into named capability predicates (one definition each, hard to forget); L1 extract the reference-gate inventory + CI coverage gate (every reference gate maps to a predicate AND an exercising test); L2 version cassettes (record wire exchange per gated op x version once, replay offline so cross-version correctness runs on EVERY PR not just nightly — extends bead 1s2); L3 live version matrix + rust-vs-python differential (done — catches server-behaviour + wrong-gate on exercised paths, release gate); L4 protocol-decoder fuzz (version-independent crash hardening). The inventory (L1) is the through-line: it forces every version-dependent decision to have a named gate and a test that proves it on the relevant versions, making missing-gate bugs impossible to ship unnoticed. Handles both parity (reference-derived gates) and our extensions (CQN — no reference, own version-scope declaration). Reference is a maintained external (23_1_EXT_3/23_4 gates exist) so L1 must re-run on reference bumps and diff for NEW gates we haven't mirrored.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE — cross-version parity hardening delivered. L1 so3w.1 (reference-gate inventory + --check-coverage CI gate + 2 below-floor 12.2 bug fixes + 34 offline boundary tests) CLOSED; L0 so3w.2 (26 gates -> 13 named version_gates predicates, behavior-identical) CLOSED; L2 so3w.3 (per-version connect cassettes + offline replay) CLOSED; L3 (live matrix + rust-vs-python truth) pre-existing + green. Version-gate bugs are now structurally unshippable: every reference gate is inventoried (CI drift-check via pin-reference), pinned by an offline boundary test (CI coverage gate), centralized in one greppable predicate module, and replayable offline per-PR. Remaining OPTIONAL/FOLLOWUP (not blocking): L4 decoder fuzz; cwsr broader post-auth op cassettes; 1s2 full reference-suite cassette replay. The strategy proved itself — the L1 audit found 2 real bugs (execute al8sqlsig, bind oaccolid) the live matrix structurally could not (below the 18c field-version floor).

title: L1: reference-gate parity audit — extract the ~56 gates, cross-check our code, hunt remaining missing/wrong gates
description: IMMEDIATE high-ROI (may find latent bugs NOW — we only fixed the gates the live matrix happened to exercise; unexercised gated paths could still be wrong). Deliver: (1) a script that extracts every version/capability gate from reference/python-oracledb thin (.pyx) into docs/reference-gates.tsv = reference-file, message-class/method, capability/constant, what byte/field is conditional (~56 rows). (2) A committed mapping: each reference gate -> our builder/parser fn + capability predicate. Account for gates we refactored into helpers (write_function_header gates the ub8 token internally — a call site with no visible if is CORRECT). (3) Cross-check each: does our code branch on the SAME capability with the SAME constant/direction? Flag suspects = missing gate OR wrong constant OR no test exercising it. (4) Verify suspects against live 18c/21c/23ai — reproduce a real bug or clear it. FIX confirmed bugs semver-additive + regression test + bead. (5) Wire a CI check: every reference-gates.tsv row must have a mapping + an exercising test (fails on a new unmapped reference gate after a bump). This is a review-assisted audit (semantic mapping, not pure static analysis; false-positive risk from refactored gates).
notes: LANDED 2026-07-05 (commit 3a90cb8): inventory (45 gates -> docs/reference-gates.tsv), extract_reference_gates.sh --check wired into CI (_quality.yml), and the 2 real bugs it found (cdeo execute.rs al8sqlsig, i0m1 bind.rs oaccolid — both missing >=12_2 write gates, below the 18c live floor, now fixed+closed). STILL in_progress: the below-floor boundaries are unit-verified only (no live server crosses them) — coverage holes = 12_1 (subscribe), 12_2/12_2_EXT1 (the two fixes), 18_1_EXT_1 (auth version-tuple, boundary==floor), supports_bool<-23_1 (minor). Close path: either add a live 12.1 lane (likely no free gvenzl 12.1 image) OR L2 cassettes (so3w.3) capturing the below-floor wire once. The CI --check gate + inventory are the durable win regardless.
close_reason: Every non-parity-neutral reference gate now has a deterministic offline per-PR boundary test, enforced by scripts/extract_reference_gates.sh --check-coverage (wired into CI, exit 4). Below-floor holes (12_1 subscribe, 12_2 describe, 18_1_EXT_1 auth, plus 12_2/12_2_EXT1 execute+bind) closed; 2 remaining suspects (supports_oob-in-CONNECT, supports_bool) verified as non-issues and reclassified PARITY-NEUTRAL. 0 code bugs. Full green bar incl version_matrix full 4 lanes + truth xe18/free23 field-by-field identical (wire unchanged).

comments.author: durakovic

ARTIFACTS: scripts/extract_reference_gates.sh (re-runnable; --check mode wired into .github/workflows/_quality.yml) + docs/reference-gates.tsv (45 gates, columns 1-3 = stable identity, 6-10 = mapping/coverage/verdict).

INVENTORY: 45 version/capability gates across all message families (execute/fetch/lob/dbobject/aq/subscribe/tpc/direct-path/auth/sessionless). lob_op/dbobject/notification/tpc_* confirmed to carry NO reference version gates (nothing to mirror).

SUSPECTS -> VERDICTS:
- 2 REAL BUGS FOUND + FIXED (both missing 12_2 write gates, both below live floor so invisible to the 18c/21c/23ai matrix):
  (1) execute.rs al8sqlsig(>=12_2)+chunk-ids(>=12_2_EXT1) written unconditionally [bead cdeo]
  (2) bind.rs oaccolid(>=12_2) written unconditionally; read side already gated it [bead i0m1]
  Both fixed semver-additive (pub(crate) threading, no public surface change), byte-identical on live matrix, unit regression tests added.
- 6 supports_* gates = PARITY-NEUTRAL (oob/fast_auth/request_boundaries/pipelining/end_user_security_context: features not implemented; conservative path writes no version-specific bytes).

GATES ALL GREEN: fmt, clippy, workspace tests (+2 new), cargo deny, semver-checks (protocol+oracledb: no update), version_matrix full 4-lane (xe11 refusal + xe18/xe21/free23 GREEN), truth xe18 + free23 (29 cases IDENTICAL rust-vs-python), extract_reference_gates.sh --check (45 gates OK).

REMAINING COVERAGE HOLES (below live floor 18c=ttc_field_version 11; no server crosses these boundaries -> feed L2 cassettes so3w.3 or add a 12.1 lane):

- 12_2 (execute/bind write [now fixed, unit-verified only] + describe read fetch.rs:756) : boundary 8 < 11

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Below-floor coverage holes CLOSED via a per-gate offline boundary-test suite + a CI coverage gate. docs/reference-gates.tsv gained column 11 boundary_test; scripts/extract_reference_gates.sh --check-coverage (wired into _quality.yml, exit 4) asserts every non-parity-neutral gate names an existing offline boundary test. Result: 34 gates pinned by offline boundary tests, 11 parity-neutral no-ops. New boundary tests (deterministic, offline, per-PR): recovery.rs auth 18_1_EXT_1 tuple layout; fetch.rs describe reads 12_2 oaccolid + 23_1 domain + 23_1_EXT_3 annotations + 23_4 vector; subscr.rs 12_1 client-id write + subscriber/instances read; aq.rs 20_1 json flag/pointer + 21_1 shard (enq/deq/array/msgprops-write + deq-props read); errors.rs 20_1 sql-type/checksum; wire.rs 23_1_EXT_1 pipeline token (function+piggyback) + MIN_LARGE_SDU framing; connect.rs ACCEPT MIN_OOB_CHECK 318 + MIN_END_OF_RESPONSE 319 + supports_oob derive; oson.rs long-field-names capability. execute/bind 12_2 + MIN_ACCEPTED reuse existing tests. EOR response-loop rows cite the ACCEPT end-of-response derivation test (wire boundary proven; consumption is plain branching). ZERO production code changed (every gated fn was already injectable): no test-hooks, no public surface change. Multi-pass bug hunt: 0 code bugs; 2 suspects reclassified to PARITY-NEUTRAL (verified non-issues): connect.pyx:111 supports_oob-in-CONNECT (supports_oob derived from ACCEPT, false at CONNECT-build; reference also emits fixed TNS_GSO_DONT_CARE; CONNECT byte-identical per live truth diff) and protocol.pyx:195,196 supports_bool (client-side validation flag, not a wire-field gate). GREEN bar: fmt; clippy -D warnings; workspace tests 0 failed; deny; semver-checks no-update for both crates; extract --check 45 gates + --check-coverage 34/11; version_matrix full 4 lanes GREEN; truth xe18 + free23 field-by-field identical 29 cases each (wire unchanged); baseline test_inventory refreshed. CI: added pin-reference.sh before the parity checks so the gitignored clean-room reference resolves in a clean checkout.

title: L0: centralize the version surface into named Capabilities predicates (one definition each)
description: Refactor scattered inline 'if ttc_field_version >= X' / 'if supports_Y' into named predicates on the Capabilities type (e.g. caps.writes_pipeline_token(), caps.end_of_response_framing(), caps.tz_file_capability()). One definition per version-decision, reached-for instead of a raw literal, so a gate is hard to forget and the set is enumerable (grep caps.<pred>). Mirrors the reference _caps.* checks 1:1. Converge opportunistically as gates are touched; write_function_header is the pattern already started.
close_reason: Centralized the version surface: 25 production ttc_field_version gates in oracledb-protocol (aq/auth/bind/connect/errors/execute/fetch/subscr/wire) + 1 in oracledb (recovery/lib) collapsed into 13 named pub(crate) const-fn predicates. 12 live in new crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin/version_gates.rs (writes_pipeline_token, carries_oaccolid, writes_al8sqlsig, writes_execute_chunk_ids, reads_error_sql_type_and_checksum, writes_aq_json_payload, carries_aq_shard_id, reads_column_domain, reads_column_annotations, reads_column_vector_metadata, writes_subscribe_client_id_block, reads_subscribe_response_details); 1 in recovery.rs (server_version_number_uses_extended_layout). Each cites the reference _caps.ttc_field_version check + constant. Pure extract-method of the CONDITION: same constant, same >= direction, no signature churn, pub(crate) only. Boundary tests/tsv/coverage untouched. All green: fmt, clippy -D, workspace tests 0-failed, deny, semver-checks both crates 'no update required', extract --check (45 gates) + --check-coverage (34 pinned/11 neutral), version_matrix full 4 lanes GREEN, truth xe18+free23 29/29 field-by-field IDENTICAL, baseline regenerated+staged.

title: L2: version cassettes — per-gated-op x version wire recordings, replay offline in CI (per-PR cross-version coverage)
description: Record the real wire exchange for each gated operation against real 11g/18c/21c/23ai ONCE (capture harness), commit as cassettes, replay offline in unit CI so a byte-level cross-version regression fails on EVERY PR in seconds with no containers. Extends bead 1s2 (cassette-replay) to be version-aware and gate-driven (drive capture from docs/reference-gates.tsv). Solves 'the live matrix is slow / needs Docker so only runs nightly'.
close_reason: L2 mechanism + first version-cassette set + offline replay + CI wiring all done and green. Capture harness (record_version_connect_cassettes, ignored/live) + offline replay (replay_version_connect_cassettes_offline, runs in cargo test -p oracledb --features cassette) live in crates/oracledb/src/version_cassettes.rs, reusing the existing record/replay transport seam. First cassette set committed for all four lanes (xe11/xe18/xe21/free23 -connect.tns-cassette + manifests): the connect-negotiation handshake (CONNECT + RESEND + real server ACCEPT), replayed offline per-PR with ReplayWriteMode::Check byte-matching the rebuilt CONNECT and ReplayAudit exact-consume, then decoding the REAL per-version ACCEPT and asserting the version-gated outcome (xe11 refusal proto 314; xe18 317; xe21 318; free23 319 + fast_auth + END_OF_RESPONSE). Scope note: the FIRST scenario is connect-negotiation, not connect+auth+query, because the auth phase uses a client OsRng session key (non-deterministic C-to-S bytes) and carries verifier/sesskey/salt secrets the leak-scanner forbids committing; the connect-negotiation surface is the richest secret-free byte-deterministic version-gated slice. Broader per-gated-op cassettes (post-auth typed query + the DPL/TPC ub8-token and CQN/SODA op gates that had the actual cross-version bugs) are tracked in the discovered-from follow-up rust-oracledb-cwsr. Gates green: fmt, clippy -D (canonical + my module 0 hits), cargo test --workspace --exclude oracledb-pyshim (0 failed), cargo test -p oracledb --features cassette incl offline replay (0 failed, proven offline/no-network), deny, semver-checks additive both crates, extract --check + --check-coverage, truth xe18+free23 field-by-field identical (behavior-neutral), baseline refreshed. Cassettes verified secret-free (only fixed placeholder CID, public service names, zero auth material). CHANGELOG + docs/RECORD_REPLAY.md section 8 added.

title: [bug] SODA SQL does not quote mixed-case descriptor column names
description: E8 R2 Pass E. soda/operation.rs:198/271 interpolated key/content/version/timestamp column names unquoted (only media type quoted) -> mixed-case mapped collections fold to uppercase + fail. FIXED: quote_ident on all descriptor columns in SELECT/INSERT/REPLACE/RETURNING. Mixed-case regressions. (SODA is experimental.)
close_reason: STALE — FIXED in E8 R2 but bead left open. Verified in current code: soda/operation.rs applies quote_ident to ALL descriptor columns [key_column :199/201, content_column :219/222, RETURNING :230/232], not just media type, so mixed-case mapped collections no longer fold to uppercase. Regressions present: mixed_case_descriptor_columns_are_quoted_in_insert_and_returning_sql [collection.rs:760], _replace_and_returning_sql [:782], _in_operation_sql [operation.rs:534]. Closing as resolved. (SODA remains experimental.)

title: Ergonomic REF CURSOR + implicit result-set consumption API (native) (oraclemcp #8)
description: From oraclemcp GH issue #8. VERIFIED current state: the low-level pieces exist and are PUBLIC — QueryValue::Cursor{cursor_id} (protocol/thin/types.rs:93), QueryResult.implicit_resultsets: Option<Vec<QueryValue>> (types.rs:555), and Connection::{scroll_cursor, release_cursor, close_cursor, define_and_fetch_rows_with_columns}. The wire/protocol path is suite-tested via the pyshim (implicit results + REF CURSOR). GAP: no ERGONOMIC high-level native API to (a) fetch rows from a returned QueryValue::Cursor without hand-managing cursor_id, (b) enumerate+fetch implicit result sets from a PL/SQL call, (c) get per-child-cursor column metadata, (d) RAII or explicit release semantics, (e) easy row/byte caps. oraclemcp emits 'unsupported' placeholders for REF CURSOR cells today. ACCEPTANCE: consume REF CURSOR OUT values + implicit result sets through stable public APIs; column metadata per child result set; bounded fetch + deterministic cleanup/release; live tests for a PL/SQL block returning a REF CURSOR and one returning implicit result sets.
close_reason: Implemented + LIVE-VERIFIED (Oracle Free 23ai). Connection::fetch_cursor(cursor: &CursorValue, max_rows) + BlockingConnection wrapper: drains a returned, self-describing REF CURSOR (a QueryValue::Cursor OUT value OR a QueryResult.implicit_resultsets entry) WITHOUT manual cursor-id handling. Column metadata on the returned QueryResult.columns (CursorValue already carries columns). First fetch = define-fetch, then fetch_rows_with_columns continuation loop (more_rows + previous_row) bounded by max_rows, releases the cursor when done. Live tests (crates/oracledb/tests/live_ref_cursor.rs): DBMS_SQL.RETURN_RESULT implicit result set -> 1-col cursor, fetch_cursor -> [1..5]; 100-row cursor bounded to 10. oraclemcp #8: agents can now consume REF CURSOR cells via a stable public API instead of placeholders.
